Let us try to be a grown-up country. Love and union between consenting adults of the same sex that is recognised in law is the least of the UK's problems.
Homosexuality is not a crime or an affliction, something to be cured or punished.
Do not judge people on their sexual oriention, but on their actions and words.
Who are you to deny love and happiness.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: Same sex marriage, as long as its consensual and does not harm the persons involved should not be banned, for what rationale is there for the banning of it other then from religious doctrine and cultral attidudes, both of which can change, are aribtrary or do not have a basis in actual factual argument. The UK should take a back step on dictating what happens with consesual relationships and rather look how the rights and safety of its citizens can be protected. The issue of same sex marriage should fit closely to how gender should not be a determining factor on how people are viewed.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: Making same sex marriage illegal would make democracy in our country take a massive step back. People within our country fought very hard to make same sex marriage legal and making this illegal persecutes gay people for being who they are and loving who they love. Everyone should be entitled to marry the person they love within a democratic country.  Same sex marriage does not hurt anyone and i believe love is love. Making same sex marriage illegal would ostracise gay people within our country and make them feel like they do not belong in the country they were born into.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text:
I strongly believe in same sex marriage. It is a basic human right to choose who our life partner is. Denying this union promotes prejudice and discrimination and we need to stand up and be united in ensuring everyone is seen as equal

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.

This would also lead to mental health challenges, self esteem issues and anxiety among this particular group.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: Making same sex marriage illegal would be against the human rights of gay people and would effectively end their rights to be in a same sex relationship. There is also no reason to make it illegal, which would make it a purely homophobic act. Making this illegal would be a slippery slope towards retracting other basic human rights on marginalised parts of society

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.

We need to work harder to eradicate the 'normal' concept of men and women marrying; same-sex relationships have always existed and always will.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: Setting aside any arguments about whether marriage in any form is useful it is absurd to suggest that same sex marriage should be illegal. This would be a moral judgment based on outdated ideas. Making same sex marriage illegal would not change the number of same sex couples in the UK but only add to the feeling of discrimination experienced by same sex couples. In practical terms my understanding is that marriage gives individuals economic and inheritance rights so that should be available to anyone in a committed relationship. This is an issue which is none of my business and nor is it the business of governments.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text:
Love should always be better than hate. Why can't people be happy for other couples finding joy with each other, no matter their genders, People should mind there own business and stop interfering with the choices of consenting adults.

There is also the equality issue and the lack of discrimination inherent in allowing same sex marriage. People are hypocrits, they don't want interference in their lives, but are quite happy to tell other people what to do with their lives when it doesn't affect them at all

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: This is a personal choice and it should not be dictated, especially to adults who can marry as long as they have consented to it. This would also be a violation of human rights. The couples who had already been married legally as same sex marriages were already legalised would mean that they have been stripped of their rights, and this will cause alarm, especially legally. These marriages promote equality and reflect the will of the people. Making it illegal would be discriminatory and damaging.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: One reason in support of making same-sex marriage illegal is that marriage has historically been between a man and a woman. This tradition has worked for well for society for millennia and is well-established and tested. We don't have as much information about the effects of same-sex marriage and it's impacts (whether positive or negative) on society. Marriage has historically had strong religious connections and it may be seen as offensive or sacrilegious to religious people to have same-sex marriage legal in the UK, especially with Christianity being the largest religion in the UK. Contrastingly, we are living in a modern society and there are now laws and rights that are associated with marriage in the UK which makes marriage something that can offer people more security and rights. Overall, as a Christian I am inclined to not be in favour of same-sex marriage but I live and let live.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text:
I am a Christian and proud of that and I understand people will have mixed views on this subject, but these days its become more acceptable to get married. but i think in todays society its all to easy to follow the pattern, my views are going to be different to other people which I do understand.
Sadly i think its a sign of the times we live in which is immoral and getting worse and standards in this country are falling rapidly which is creating so much upheaval and one day we will all be judged on how we have decided to live our lives.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: I think marriage should remain traditional between a man and a woman. That is how the original concept was however it has been changed to accommodate this new modern idea even though most people associate a marriage between a man and a woman in their head. I also think that most people are pressured to accept a concept that they might not personally agree with. I have noticed that certain groups and identities are given more visibility which makes it unequal when you compare it to other groups or identities so this is where I think we need to reconsider marriage in general.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.

in any shape or form.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text:
Marriage doesn't mean much to most people nowadays. Children aren't brought up in 2 parent families anymore and couples rarely stay together for the sake of their children. Times are different and there is no longer a sense of shame felt by people. Living 'over the brush' used used to be frowned upon, and there would be pressure on people from friends, family and society to confirm to societal norms. I understand that this way of thinking is still prevalent within certain communities, but not mine. Marriage doesn't mean very much to most people like me, so the sex of the couples is irrelevant.

I have long ago ceased to care what other people do with their lives as long as it doesn't impact me, and isn't harmful. I'm not religious, so I have no problems with same sex marriage from that perspective. As long as they don't impinge on my happiness and freedom, then I'm willing to live and let live.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: I totally disagree that the UK should make same sex marriage illegal.  In the present day, with sexuality being so open, who are we to tell people who they can love and marry.  As long as it is a genuine love marriage then I personally do not see the issue.  The law should be changed to reflect the world we live in now and allow people to be happy and marry who they wish to.  It really is a matter for the individual couples to decide if they wish to marry and it should be the as when heterosexual people marry.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: John Stuart Mill captured the basic argument concerning this issue in the nineteenth century. Society should avoid limiting the freedom of the individual to those situations where the freedom of one person impacts on the freedom of another in exercising their rights. Legislating to abolish same-sex marriage impacts on the right of the individual. The provision of same sex marriage has no impact on the rights of this who might have strong views against this arrangement. It is when people try to force their views on others that we descend into the realm of the totalitarian state and the sad consequences of that.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: There is no public consensus for making same-sex marriage illegal.  It is not a topic that anybody is talking about.  The fact is that same-sex marriage is currently legal and therefore why would you seek to make it illegal.  It does not make sense.   We are not in. the dark ages and this would be a very regressive step for society.  I dont believe that anybody would be in support of this other than some far right bigots.  It would need to through parliament anyway and Labour certainly allow this through as a topic for discussion in parliament.  A non starter.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: I recognise that this issue can be a significant one for certain parts of society - particularly religious groups.  However, to me this seems like a total non-issue.  It is a long time since homosexuality was illegal - this seems to me to be quite right.  So following on from that, if 2 people love one another and are part of a legal relationship, then it seems only fair for them to have the same right to marry as heterosexual couples. Discrimination based around someone's sexuality seems to me to be completely wrong, and I am happy to live somewhere where this is considered the norm.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: I strongly support this issue in order to preserve traditional values and norms in the UK. Marriage is rooted in religious and cultural traditional which same- sex marriage would go against. It should be for a man and a woman and or raising children, otherwise there is no need for it. Same sex marriage imposes on the morals and cultures adopted by many citizens. Making a change in the law would better reflect the viewpoint of many people in the UK,  and maintain a structure of tradition in the UK and keep the sanctity of marriage as it was intended.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.
rows.text: I strongly agree that the UK should make same-sex marriage illegal. Marriage, as a bedrock institution, must be preserved in its traditional form-exclusively between a man and a woman-to maintain social stability and honour centuries of cultural and religious heritage. Legal recognition of same-sex unions coercively challenges religious groups, threatening their autonomy to practice faith-based teachings on marriage. Furthermore, children's well-being is best served by dual-gender parenting, which provides complementary maternal and paternal influences essential for balanced development. Redefining marriage disregards those biological and sociological realities, prioritizing individual desires over social health. Upholding traditional marriage protects foundational values, religious liberty, and the interest of future generations.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.

Although I do understand that with the world heading in a certain direction more and more people are becoming gay, perhaps to make a stance for themselves and provisions need to be made for them such as legal union/civil partnership but I believe that marriage is a holy thing and shouldn't be provided to same sex couples.

rows.proposition: The UK should make same-sex marriage illegal.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: As someone who does not have nor want kids, I am against any incentive for higher birth rates. This would mean I have to pay even more towards the upkeep of all these kids. With welfare, benefits, credits, education, special needs it is incredibly expensive. Not to mention all the benefits that people who have kids get, with free days off, maternity leave, fee nursery places. I also think the world is overpopulated and we do not need to increase this. Resources are running out, natural habitats of many animals are being destroyed to make way for humans. I also do not know why anyone would want to bring kids into a world at present so uncertain.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:
The current rates of immigration to the UK mean that our population is rising, that together with the fact that once here the immigrant families rapidly increase in size due to child birth means that we do not need to have an incentive. Offering incentives will mean that the wrong people are having larger families just to gain the incentive, what is there in place to support these families longer term and just how is the government going to be able to afford this?
There are rising incidences of child neglect in the UK currently, by incentivising larger families you are inviting more children being born into poverty and neglect and becoming a drain on the welfare system

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:
There is no evidence to suggest that a larger population would result in a higher quality of life. This appears to be solely based on increasing economic output rather than measuring the true impact on the quality of life for all species on earth.  Overcrowding tends to lead to higher pollution, environmental degradation and faster depletion of the earth's resources.

If the UK were to incentivise higher birth rates this would place a greater demand on the finite resources available within the UK. As it is the UK does not have enough housing or high quality jobs to support a surge in the population. In addition there are other resources such as healthcare & education which are already struggling so would not be able to cope. Not to mention the UK is unlikely to be able to produce enough food or energy to support a larger population.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: The government should not get involved in how many children people decide to have. This is because it should remain the choice of the individual and not any government. If the government was to incentivise having bigger families the birth rate could result in there being too many people in society.There could be strain but on the health service, education and general infrastructure if people are encouraged to have more children. In addition, this policy could be seen as discriminatory towards those unable to have children. It is morally wrong to incentivise people to have more or indeed less children in a civilised society.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:
To put it bluntly, not all sectors of the population contribute equally to the economy. By incentivising population are we in danger of encouraging the lowest contributors, who may not have come from a culture of independence and strong work ethics, to add to the population without solving the issues. Yes we need more people of working age to support an aging population, but adding to the unemployment figures helps no-one, especially if there isn't a corresponding increase in education and training resources. We may be better looking at better training for those currently out of work rather than adding to the pool without a corresponding support system.

Where precisely would these extra people go? The world population is growing at an unsustainable rate and the UK itself cannot accommodate current population growth so how would this work?

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: Child birth should be a completely natural thing with no outside influence whatsoever. The Government should not need to get involved in this area at all. Child birth should certainly not be incentivised, that just encourages raising more children for other personal gains. These gains also affect the welfare system and bigger families mean more welfare payouts and bigger houses needed to be built. An encouraging child birth policy by Government could mean that there would be a new culture of people wanting children because of other benefits given to them. It could lead to a more demanding society, more money grabbing, more selfish society

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: The Earth's natural resources are already under massive strain due to ever-accelerating population growth across the world (except in the limited number of countries experiencing population decline). Why would we then seek to increase the population further?! Not only natural resources but also social, economic and human ones (such as jobs, already being taken by AI, and housing) would become in short supply if the population were to increase even further. And this is without mentioning the fact that pro-natal policies move towards eugenics polices -- trying to shape a population. The implications for equality and welfare would be very concerning.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.

My last point, is that the world has so many conflicts now, between countries, between people, many previously safe communities are having more crime on the streets, the world in general is less safe now.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: I don't fully agree with this point because people choose not to have as many or any children depending on their own situation. Some people may not be able to financially afford a child, living situation etc. Having an incentive may increase people to have more children without thinking of the long term if this is a good idea for them individually. I think this would cause the opposite effect of a negative situation compared to a positive situation in society. If people had kids they can't afford more strain on the government for benefits and other factors that need to be considered in this situation.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: I do not think that the UK should be implementing policies to incentivise higher birth rates as I think an increase in birth rates and population would negatively impact our country. Increased birth rates would increase the population in an already overpopulated country, leading to even more pressure on public services, more competition in the job market and therefore higher unemployment rates, more pressure on benefit systems and more pressure on UK infrastructure. The UK already spends a lot of its public spending on child benefits, so increasing the birth rate would mean that even more money would be spent.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:
The decision to have a baby is a very personal one and there should be no state interference. Unfortunately it could mean that people would have children for financial gain and this could be costly for society as a whole as it would impact on both education and the NHS. It could, in effect have the opposite of the desired effect.
We must not ignore those parents who are unable to have children and their views must be taken into account. What financial rewards do they receive?

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:
Increase in the general population may lead to overcrowding if this policy to incentivize higher birth rate is being implemented in the UK and this can indirectly affect public services like education, housing, transportation and healthcare because the may become too small for these services to accommodate the rising population. This can however lead to pressure on these services and systems and cause a decline in the quality of service being rendered to each persons. A significant investment on infrastructure should follow up to avoid or mitigate the struggle in accommodating the growing population.

Secondly, offering financial aids or incentives to encourage childbirth can lead to poor support from the parent, such that these parents are just after the money the receive and not considering their long-term support to the child, so their priority is to have more children just to get the money at that time.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:

Plus what about the new schools needed, new doctors, new dentists etc. these sectors are in a mess now and will just get worse without funding into them first.

How will you stop these births from those on benefits with no desire to work or pay back into society and just using this policy as an excuse to not work for longer and claim off the state.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.

Due to an aging population there needs to be an increase in birth rates to maintain the population and stop it from declining. Future generations will be the ones who in time will run the country from politics to the work force. 
To keep staff numbers in workforces high, there needs to be a constant stream of staff to avoid staff shortages and keep stress at bay. Staff shortages can have a toll on peoples mental health due to the higher workload that they have to deal with. Also, the older staff members will be taking retirement and will need replacing.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:
If young people choose to not have children or have them decades later than our ancestors did in the past, choosing to also have fewer children, we will eventually find ourselves in a situation where older people far outnumber younger people and once these older individuals come to the end of their lives, we may only have half the population numbers in some countries. This may continue to get worse and who knows what the ramifications are of this.
 I also believe that children who grow up with siblings tend to be more well rounded individuals and have better support networks than only children. They learn to play well, cooperate in groups, share with others, empathise better and can be less narcissistic.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text:
I think that it would be a good ide overall to incentivise people to increase their birth-rate and produce more children. A higher birth rate would increase our workforce and tax intake in years to come. Incentivising a higher birth rate also ensures that we have a more youthful population in years to come, population imbalance is not a healthy sign of a country overall and the morale of a countries work force and people overall increases with more kids in the population.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: I think that the low birth rate, in conjunction with economic policies predicated on the need for continuous growth, currently necessitates high levels of immigration. High levels of immigration weaken the core culture of the nation, and are thus problematic. The "sense of togetherness" felt by citizens of a culturally diverse nation is less than the "sense of togetherness" felt by citizens with similar moral and ethical principles. An increased birth rate would decrease the need for immigration. Ultimately, I think such a policy (incentivised higher birth rate) is desirable from the governments point of view, and would strengthen the state. As an individual, the benefits of a strong state are less tangible and relevant.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: The economy will be hit badly in the future if the population has dropped too much if there are no high birth rates then no new humans. Childcare is very expensive right now another thing the policy should help with maybe there can be a benefit that can help young mothers out at an affordable price.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: We are facing a crisis of an aging population, with the consequences of this being a higher burden on the state to provide healthcare, pensions, etc. To combat this, we need to grow the economy which can be helped by having more people working and expanding industries to produce greater GDP. If the working population is larger, this will increase the tax revenues available to help fund the problem of having an aging population. The UK needs to expand it's workforce to be able to compete more on the world stage. Younger people are better able to advance the country based on the everchanging requirements of new technologies and the speed of change.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.
rows.text: It is not something i have ever thought about before but perhaps it would be beneficial if we are facing a population issue. An we need more people to be working and to be in society. rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to incentivise higher birth rates.

rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.

rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.
rows.text:
Again, the proposal is a noble one, but in reality, not a practical target. 

To carry out such a proposal, the UK's energy grid would need to be upgraded. We would have to build large battery storage facilities across the country. The costs to have all of this planned and completed within five years would be unimaginable. 

The UK already has a large amount of debt, which we play huge amounts of interests on. Adding to that figure to carry out such a proposal would results in the government having to take away large amounts of money from essential services like the NHS and our schools. This could result in the collapse of such services and social anarchy. 

Finally, it's also worth pointing out that such a plan could leave the UK vulnerable when there are periods of weather that don't suit renewables (i.e. still, overcast weather). Leaving the country solely reliant on renewables could result in blackouts, which again, could results in the collapse of essential services and social anarchy.

rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.

Also, with other countries around the world who still have working coal mines, it not only makes the UK governments efforts futile, but quite simply pointless.  For this reason alone, it's highly unachievable.

rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.

Even the move towards electric vehicles seems to be stuttering with the government stopping the road tax relief which was originally in place. Also it seems that public confidence in electric vehicles appears to be diminishing somewhat. While an important issue, especially for energy security, its unreasonable to set unreachable targets.

rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.

The reasons for being supportive of this is that I am gravely concerned about global warming and we need to transition to more renewable energy as a matter of urgency. rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.

rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.

rows.proposition: The UK should transition to 100% renewable energy by 2030.

This is a policy for the world to take on board and not just the UK and we do still have some considerable influence on the world stage and toavert climate change will take a tidal change throughout the major economies of the world. If we dont seriously adopt such policies I think we also risk being left behind in terms of green innovation and the technologies that need to be developed. Whatever the current politic, one thing is clear. climate change is not going away action is imperitive. Unfortunately I think 5 years is too short a tie and there is little hope of achievin a target of 100% renwable energy in time for 2030 however we should accelerate towards such a policy and target with a view to reaching it as soon as possible, even if we cannot get there before 2030.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: We live in an ever changing world and employees, certainly in a professional capacity, have to undertake continuous professional development to stay up to date with their jobs. We don't have to understand how or why someone feels like they do but we should just appreciate and accept that they do. Who are we to say how someone should feel.  Therefore we should make every effort to include them and make them feel better if we are able. If this requires training by employers then so be it. I

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: I think that a level of diversity and inclusion training is a good thing for employees so that everyone has the same training and is aware of the different aspects of diversity and inclusion.  It gives everyone the same opportunity to understand the different types of people they may be working with.  Some people may not have worked with any minorities before and may be unsure how they are to be treated.  Inclusion means that everyone has the same opportunities whoever they are and whatever their background is and they cannot be discriminated against for this.  It is just treating everyone the same and helping people to carry this out.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: I believe it's a good thing for UK employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees. It's very good to remind people of the importance of being diverse and inclusive, even though they might have heard it many times. The reason I am not 100% in agreement with the statement is because it can be dragged out and once people start to get bored of the message, they might start to get annoyed by it and that could give the opposite result. Make it mandatory, but make it short and snappy and relevant to each particular employment team and sector.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: I think if employers were forced to implement mandatory d&i training for all employees, this could potentially produce more negaitves than positive. There would be a significant cost and resource burden on companies. Whilst for the larger companies this would probably not have much on an impact, on smaller companies this could be the difference between taking on a new employee or not. So it could massively negatively affect them. Workers/employees could potentially feel that their freedom of speech is being affected. Whilst i do not condone any negativity towards this subject. People need to be allowed to make their own choices. On the positive side, it could potentially help employers be protected against legal risks and allow them to comply to the equality act.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: I do think some kind of training is a good thing but I had to do a training course every year where I worked and that is just 'overkill'.  Maybe the training could be online or in quiz form to actually determine people's understanding.  I was shocked when I first did training as I admit I didn't really know what diversity was but once you understand there is no need to keep drumming it in.  I think a lot of staff do training and dont' really take it in so maybe a better approach is actually test whether people understand what diverisyt and inclusion is.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: Making diversity and inclusion training mandatory for all employees might not be the best approach for every workplace. While the intention behind such training is positive, it can take valuable time away from employees focusing on learning the specific skills they need to do their jobs well. In many cases, these sessions may not fit well with the culture or needs of different teams or industries, which can cause some employees to feel uncomfortable, out of place, or even resistant to the training. This discomfort can sometimes lead to tension within teams rather than promoting understanding. Additionally, these training programs often require a lot of time and resources to organize and deliver, which could be used more effectively to improve work processes or support professional development. As a result, mandatory training might lower overall productivity and efficiency. Although promoting diversity and inclusion is very important, forcing a one-size-fits-all training on all employees could have unintended negative effects and may not be the most effective way to achieve those goals.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text:
If you live in a city in the last 60 years you'll have grown up in a diverse world; chances are your friends are of varying races. You don't need someone telling you how "we're all the same" but that if someone is different, no matter how offensive to our values, it's their cultural identity and can't be criticised. Training tends to put any personal incompatability or issues down to race. It's alright not to get on with someone of your own race, but if that person is of another race, then you're being racist. This causes perception of injustice.

Inclusion training becomes an issue in that it puts unreasonable demands on employers in non-diverse areas. Where I live  is almost exclusively white so failing to employ a diverse workplace will be seen as poor training rather than a fact of life in the area.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: Politics has no place in the workplace, and diversity and inclusion training is a form of political action. Making it mandatory forces people to adopt opinions that they may not personally agree with. Individuals have the right to their own personal opinions, even if others find them unpleasant or offensive. It is a form of authoritarianism to mandate viewpoints. Personally, I am a feminist and am broadly tolerant of diverse gender identities, but I do not expect others to share my views, viewing efforts to force my views on others as childish behaviour, more appropriate for the schoolyard than the workplace. True tolerance includes the ability to accept that others may legitimately have views that differ from your own, and that they have the right to keep those views private, if they wish. You cannot mandate true tolerance, you can only mandate sycophancy.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.

It would help ensure work places are welcoming places and set up correctly to accomodate individuals regardless of their background. The only potential down side is the cost involved in implenting this but I think if  it was done in the right way and tone was right then I think it would be a very positive step forward for our society.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text:
I fully support diveristy and inclusion programmes and I would hope employers across the country would be happy to include programmes to educate their employees on the benefits of a diverse and inclusive workplace. First off, 'diversity and inclusion' does NOT just include people or colour or LGBTQ+ people. It includes everyone: people with neurodivergencies, people with disabilities or impairements as well as people of all ethnicities. Having a diverse and inclusive workplace ensures ALL voices are heard, not just the same voices all of the time. The programmes can lead to an organisation excelling and becomine a very desirable place to work.
However, making these programmes mandatory could likely bring resentment to those who have to take part. They may see it as another task or training programme to benefit someone other than them, but in reality a diverse and inclusive workplace should be a given everywhere.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: I think many people assume that DEI protects minorities, but I think it also protects companies as it forces them to actually hire people based on their qualifications and not white privilege. I think there's part of it that protects the ego of fragile white male candidates who lack qualifications, who use it as an excuse as to why they haven't been hired over their female counterpart who actually fits the role. A diverse environment promotes productivity and innovation as people from different backgrounds with different experiences offer different perspectives. Ignorance is a cause of bigotry and implementing a mandatory DEI training will also help employees to broaden their horizons and make them better people.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: The law would create a better educated workforce and encourage development of their understanding around D+I. This would reduce discrimination and stereotyping and lead to a happier work force, it should also reduce workplace conflicts. As a result, the workforce are likely to be happier as there is less chance of outdated points of view. This is also likely to benefit companies and the economy as a whole as a happier workforce are more productive and will deliver better results. The only concerns I have are about how easy it would be to ensure everyone has received the training in accordance with the law.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
rows.text: I believe that diversity and inclusion training should be mandatory for all employees on balance, because training could help to educate employees who may not know a lot or have direct experience of prejudice and discrimination. Many of us believe that we do not discriminate, but there is always the potential that we hold unconscious biases that impact our interactions with those from minority communities. Training helps to shine a light on lived experiences of those who have faced discrimination, and this may encourage employees to open up with one another and discuss their experiences plainly. This could result in empathy and understanding being built between employees. Training also sets the culture in the company that prejudice and discrimination are not acceptable. The reason I didn't rate this higher was because I'm aware of tokenism, and that sometimes employees from minority communities don't want a spotlight shone on them.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.

However I believe this training is important since it promotes fairness in the workplace. The training creates a balance environment where it creates awareness of discrimination faced by some groups. Through the training there will be more inclusivity in decision making and all the employees will get equal treatment irrespective of their background. This will in turn increase individual and team productivity. Training ensures team members are equip with the necessary skills thus increasing creativity. The training could however be a burden to small organisation due to the cost involved.

rows.proposition: The UK should require employers to implement mandatory diversity and inclusion training for all employees.
