rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: In agreeance with this point, I think this policy simply enforces the idea of basic human rights that everyone should have access to. In protection and promotion of religious freedoms, Muslim communities will have the freedom of speech and expression that other communities, such as Christian communities, have already had for a long time. There have been many instances where Muslim communities have faced instances of prejudice and danger due to the expression of their religious practices; this policy creates space and safety to allow the Muslim communities to stand on equal ground with other religious communities, able to freely practice their religion.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: The UK should implement policies to protect and promote religious freedoms for Muslim communities. Many Muslims still face discrimination and challenges when trying to practice their faith, whether it’s wearing religious clothing, finding prayer spaces, or observing religious holidays. These barriers can make people feel excluded or misunderstood. By protecting religious freedom, the UK can help build a more inclusive and respectful society where everyone feels accepted. It’s not just about supporting one group, it’s about making sure everyone has the same rights and opportunities, no matter their religion. Stronger policies would show that the UK truly values diversity and equality

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: There is no need to promote any religions through state intervention; the idea of a future Britain should be one aiming for secular habits.  The Islamic communities are already highly supported, to the detriment of pretty much every other minority group, and to say they need singling out for protection in this anti-Semitic world is childish.  Islam has on the whole done a great deal more harm than good for Britain - and the world entire, come to that - and should not be encouraged and promoted in such a fashion.  Islam is indeed counter-British and anti-Britain, and should if anything be discouraged.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: Each and every religion should be treated exactly the same. Starting to treat one differently to the rest is treading a very dangerous path. One religion is no different to any other at all in any way. There are far more important things policy makers need to concentrate on rather than making problems where there are none.  This would be a needless use of time and resources that would be much better off being spent on things that matter to everybody. For example to the national health service, more police officers or on making all the roads in England better.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text:
Religious freedoms should not be protected by the state - particularly Mulim religious practices which are contrary to freedoms of the wider population.  Non stun halal slaughter for example is contrary to western society's prevention of cruelty to animals.

Islamic segregation of sexes and consideration of women as lesser than males is also incompatible with a western democratic society.

Government should protect the rights of wider society in preference to those of an Islamic minority.  Those wishing to live under Islamic laws can relocate to the many Islamic countries which can accomadate their beliefs.  Acceptance of Islamic religious laws is detrimentatl to UK society.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: I think that the Uk should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedom for Muslim communities as we have to address anti Muslim hate.  The government should actively challenge anti Muslim speech and sentiment to ensure that it is not acceptable or tolerated in public oration or institutions.  I think that the government should create educational initiatives and public awareness campaigns to help create greater understanding and respect between different religious and non religious communities.  This would create much needed harmony in communities.  Although the government has existing laws against hate crime, they need to be effectively enforced with strict investigations and prosecution of perpetrators.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: I strongly agree with this statement and nothing will change my mind regarding this. I have family members who are muslim and friends and they are some of the best people I know. Everyone should be allowed to practise their faith and not being allowed to goes against the cultural values of the Uk. In truth many muslims are discriminated against, probably the most of any group in the Uk and for such a large group the negativity and hatred is very high and unreasonable. For people arguing that they do not feel safe, then they should put themselves in the shoes of southeast asains and muslims and understand how unsafe people like us feel.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: All Muslim communities should be able to pray to who they believe in, we shouldn't dictate to them as everyone as a right on their own. Muslims should be able to be free and live their lives accordingly no one should be able to take this away from them. If Muslims want to pray then this is also their right to do so. If they want to fast for a religious purpose then they should also do so. If they want to go to the Mosques then no one as a right to stop them unless things are done in an unlawful manner everyone should be able to practice whatever religion they wish to do.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: Ensuring the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for muslim communties in the UK is about upholding the values of fairness, respect and inclusion. At its core, religious freedom is a fundamental human right, allowing individuals to practice their faith without fear of discrimination. when governments actively safeguard these freedoms, the help buld stronger social togertherness, bringing understanding and unity among diverse communities. Unfortunately prejudice and systematic challenges still exists, and without clear protections, muslims may face unncecessary barriers in education, employment and public life. Strong policies send messages that discrimination has n place in the society and that every individual regardless of faith deserves equal opportunities.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: So I think everyone should have legal protection and the right to pursue whatever religious beliefs they choose whether it be Islam or those who claim to be Jedi Knights and channel the Force. However, I don't favour the promotion of Islamic beliefs in the same way I don't favour the promotion of any religious beliefs as many people are not followers or religion and have little in the way of religious beliefs. Whilst people should be free to pursue religious freedoms they should not compromise or put themselves existing secular laws. For example there should be attempts to enforce blasphemy laws through the back door. Protection of religious freedoms should go hand in hand with protection for those who have no interest in any form of religion.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: The uk is a chrstian nation with a rich history of faith and anglicanism/ catholicism, this should be preserved and considered as the tue identity of the country, while it has seen a lot of migration in the past century, its important to consider what is british, and how that relates to faith also as it relates to the inherent identity of the country and to avoid changing this, while also considering the needds of others. Also, not relgion should have more feedoms or protections than the other, and in general religions should be regulated more by law, to the point where they are given less freedoms and ability to influence choices people and agendas in people, and especially in children. Fundamtally the world we live in and have built as a civilisation is a result of the scientific proccess, and the destruction of the importance of religion

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: The UK should make sure that Muslims are able to practice their religion freely and safely. Whether or not policies need to be implemented to ensure this is another matter. As UK citizens we are free to practice our religions without fear of persecution and this is the case for the UK's Muslim population. Some time their religion comes under attack from certain elements of the wider population and it is important that minority groups are protected and can feel safe to practice their religion without fear of any violence or persecution. It is the right thing to do in a multicultural society.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: As I have stated above, I believe in a fundamental right to freedom of speech, expression and religion. However, this must be by it's very nature limited and subordinate to to the rights and freedoms of others who may not share that religious belief. In the case of Islam it is on a fundamental level, not very liberal and is unique in the sense that contained within it is a sociology-political ideology in many cases. I would not support this being "promoted" as there are values intrinsic to Islam in many cases that would infringe on the rights of women for example.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: I believe that UK institutions do already implement several policies to protect religious freedoms for recognised religions, this includes observance and freedom. In some ways I do agree with the statement but I don't understand why we are singling out one religion? Im unsure on the definition of "promotion", it sounds wrong to me and dangerous. There is also some issues with some religions preach or are interpreted in ways that are contrary to law so its a sticky problem. In my view all religions should be banned and kept as a hobby and not treated in a special way.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text:
Islam is fast becoming the boogey man in Great Britain, people are poor and angry and looking for someone to righteously hate. in 1930s Germany it was the Jews, in modern Britain it's Muslims. We already have legislation in place that protects freedom of religion in the UK, we just need to make sure that they are applied correctly.
If the government was to try and intervene with the introduction of specific legislation that just protects Muslims, we would be inviting what could essentially become a very violent reaction from the British public indeed. I see no need to poke the bear and tempt fate at this very uncomfortable time in our history.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: I am slightly torn on this subject. Yes, I agree that people shouldn't have to worry about hatred towards their religion (realistically, this is unfortunately one of the most common reason for wars breaking out in the world. However whilst I agree that people should be able to promote their religion to those who are interested in it, i do not agree with promoting religion in public in a way that it is trying to convert you there and then. If, say, I am out shopping I do not suddenly want someone waving a book in front of me telling to that this is what my beliefs should now be.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement policies to ensure the protection and promotion of religious freedoms for Muslim communities.
rows.text: I think that by allowing inmates to practice their religious beliefs is appropriate. We need to be tougher on time served and it should be used as wiping the slate clean and re programming offenders, All privileges should be withdrawn and they should be all living under the same rules and practices. We are simply too soft in this country and we need to toughen up. Some see prisons as a social place, a place to be with the boys and simply dont take it seriously. It should be a deterrent in itself that religious freedoms arent allowed in prisons.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text:
Forcing people to vote is not very democratic and shows how governments can slowly start to overstep their authority and try and impose means to control the population in order to try and get a favourable result during any given election.

Especially as fewer and fewer people are voting these days as politics is a game of lies, who can tell the biggest/most lies during an election only to go back on these "promises" aka lies once the have been elected into power.

There are better ways to try and entice more people to vote, mandatory voting is not one of those ways!

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: While voting is a needed process for democracy, making it mandatory for everyone to vote, could cause many issues. Firstly, people who live abroad will have to go through the stressful process of figuring out voting from abroad, or need to travel back to the UK to do so. That means using time, effort, and money in order to vote, making this an unpleasant, stressful event, which people need less of in their lives. Secondly, people with disabilities, be it physical of mental, may find this troublesome. I speak of this as someone with disabilities myself: some people may struggle considerably to go through with voting, as it's not an easy, seamless process, which more often than not requires physical presence. Lastly, forcing people to vote takes away people's choice and forces them into one. What happens when someone does not have political affiliation or opinion? They probably either vote randomly, or follow the generic sentiment. Democratic processes are about choice, and this would take away from it.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: There is no good reason to compel people to vote in elections, and in a free society , we should be free to not vote of we wish.  Many people have no interest in politics, or don't support any party, so it would be pointless demanding that they vote.  Practically this would be impossible to arrange.  Many people would be physically unable to vote, by reason of illness, or absence.  This is an unnecessarily draconian measure, which would have no benefit to the democratic process, and would only serve to alienate the ordinary person.  What would the penalties be for non-compliance?

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: The problem with mandatory voting in the UK, or anywhere else to be honest is the limited choice offered by political parties and the electoral system we use. It is often difficult to discern how policy proposals differ from one party to another and further to understand how that might affect the man in the street. There is also a lack of transparency around policy impacts. It is therefore important that people should be allowed to choose whether they want to exercise their right to vote or not vote to express a belief about the current political system. Implementing mandatory voting would be at best extremely expensive but from a practical point of view I doubt very much it is possible to do.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: Having voting become mandatory would feel like a betrayal of my rights as an individual. The while purpose of voting is to encourage choice and having it forced onto you would completely reduce the whole experience to an obligation and likely effect the way in which we vote. How would this be informed and what sort of punishments would incur if I didn't vote? I believe it is important to let individuals decide their lives for themselves and have the abiltiy to make their own minds up. Voting is just one of those things that needs to be protected always.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: If more people vot we get a much more balanced split between the various parties. People complain that the government does not represent them but  by having to vote they would hopefully feel more connected and involved. By having a large turnout it would be much hardwe for a few people to influence others and cause voting glitches. Our first past the post system is very unfair when only a small proportion of people vote for that party while many who oppose them sit at home doing nothing. We need to make more people vote to ensure everyone gets a say in how we live.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: In essence, it is a person's own choice whether to vote or not but we all have the privilege of voting so we should take that privilege and use it. It's very easy to not make the journey to the local polling station because you can't be bothered but there are some people in the world who could dream of being able to vote the way UK residents can. Choosing not to vote means that a prime minister will be elected not based on the wishes of the UK. We need as many people voting rather than 80% of the population; every vote counts.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: For a democratic state to be truly democratic the vote needs to be truly representative of the whole of the general population. Some communities and demographics are largely represented in voting as they will turn out to vote or make use of the postal voting service. Other demographics are largely unrepresented as many do not vote. Therefore some councils and governments are voted in by a low majority due to low voting numbers. A mandatory vote would be good to counter balance this low representation, but them schools would need to educate young people on political issues so that as they reached voting age, they can make an informed vote.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text:
I think it should be made mandatory for several reasons:  

Some would be voters are too apathetic to vote but they should have a say as everyone should have representation regardless of their political leanings.  We would get voters from a wide range of demographics if it were compulsory.  There is no excuse as can send in a postal vote so whoever you are can vote assuming you are eligible.  It should mean a fair representation and it really would be the people's vote as currently the system is very flawed and shocking how few people actually vote, yet still moan if the party they ideally would lie do not get in!

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: There should always be freedom of choice, as thus country stands for this. forcing poele to choose especially whne they do not support any part is wrong and a waste of votes. It will also put too much effort and resources to apply this and put into action. Thus I do not believe it is right and worth doing. On the other hand, I do believe, people who choose not to vote, should not moan and complain. You have to be able to take part in order to see change. its an opprtunity to have your say and maybe make a difference.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: We are at a point now where many people won't vote as we cannot see anything improving. This is due to the state in which the country is in. We are in cost of living crisis and the governments we have been dealing with have made things worse. It is increasingly getting more and more stressed and less people are willing to take a chance on the parties. We no longer faith in them. Another thought is that we are monitored already in every way and are getting sick of being told what to do when a lot of politicians do not seem to abide to what they are proposing we do.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: Its not a good idea not everyone likes to vote or are political .There are people not able to vote they are sick mentally unwell have dementia .Its not ideal that you have to produce ID not everyone wants to produce ID .I think having to provide ID is really bad it is supposed to be a secret vote so now people can see who voted for who.Mentally ill people would not be appropriate to vote as who would be voting them or ther carers  .Its not ideal to do mandatory voting only let people vote who want to vote

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: I believe mandatory voting should be introduced as presently I feel that the majority of voters are from the wealthier, better educated & dare I say white conservative group. The lower, poorer social classes tend to abstain from voting believing that all political groups are much the same & that they cannot change things by casting votes. This of course leads to the lower classes being underrepresented in parliament. This of course leads to the case that the lower social groups are not taken into consideration as much as their wealthier  counterparts by the political party in charge who naturally wish to pander to the social group who voted them into power.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: I believe it should be mandatory to vote in all major elections in the UK for everyone over the age of 18 - with some exceptions.  There will be many over 18s who do not have the mental capacity to be able to decide who should be in power and people like this - such as those with conditions such as dementia or alzheimers or a brain condition/injury should not have to vote.  Also, people in prison should lose their right to the vote.  I believe the vote should only be open to those born in the UK or who have lived in the UK for a set amount of time and have acquired British citizenship. However, for the majority of UK citizens I think it is only right that they should have to vote as then there can be no complaints from them about who is in power.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: i think its important that everyone gets to state their opinion and gets the chance to vote my second point was that if people don't vote they will not have an input and this can lead to their votes been missed and not been counted this can result in the votes actually coming in wrong and  not  actually been what the general public seriously want. i know polling stations are open a good few hours but things happened and emergencies etc the does stop people from actually coming to vote. maybe the window should be extended for exceptional circumstances maybe.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: I believe voting is important but I have been in a position myself where I’ve not voted.  This was out of apathy and distrust of all parties equally.  Almost like they were the same old hiring different shades of the same colour telling the same lies and spinning the same half truths.  It seemed like no choice I could make was good.  That being said I can try to see the positives in voting even if my policy sometimes is which party do I dislike the least.  Sad but true.  I also try to vote on local issues aswell as national.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: I sometimes vote. I sometimes don't vote. I don't have very much political opinion about anything because I don't feel politics is something to get overly involved in. All parties are the same to be honest. Some countries around the world fight to have the right to vote and so perhaps my ambivalence is wrong. Maybe then it should be implemented. Maybe everyone should be told to vote and then the result will be a true one. It really will be the voice of the people because everyone voted. I guess though there's always the issue of what happens to those who do not vote because of illness perhaps.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text: The idea of the democratic election process is that people should have the choice of who they would like to vote for. However there will be many situations where a voter might look at the proposed candidates and decide that none of them are worth voting for. Forcing people to vote would mean that they would vote for someone even if they didn't want them as their elected representative. The only way this could be obviated would be is people could place a tick for 'no one' but that would defeat the object of mandatory voting. It should also be noted that the cost of administering this process would likely be prohibitive.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement mandatory voting in all major elections.
rows.text:
Since everyone is affected by the policies implemented by the government everyone should have their voice heard. However I'd counter that those who have no interest or undestanding shouldn't vote. Personally I believe every adult should have the right to vote if they wish to do so. However they should demonstrate that they understand what they are voting for as currently it's to easy for politicians and the media to manipulate the uneducated, uninformed and gullible into voting in support of one side. A good example is the 2016 Referendum on leaving the EU. The leave campaign wanted to leave the EU but had no plan of what to do afterwards and didn't propose an alternative vision. Voting data shows that 70% of people who left school at 16 voted to Leave and 68% of those with degrees voted to Remain. Many of those who voted for Brexit didn't understand what it was about, they just felt the current system ignored them and wanted something different in the vain hope that things would improve. They didn't understand they were voting away their hard won rights and protections or to make themselves poorer and for less funds for public services. or that Brexit was thought up by the far-right think tank of 55 Tufton Street and funded by greedy and unscrupulous US billinaires like Robert mercer and the Koch brothers and was about ditching EU controls that protect ordinary people but reduce corpoate profit for the super rich elite. If the electorate had been made aware of these things I suspect many who voted to leave would have changed their minds.

My alternative proposal is that every adult has the right to vote, but must demonstrate they understand what they're voting for. To do this they would take a multiple choice quiz about the policies of each party on major issues including: the economy, the NHS, social care, climate change, education, defense, law 7 order, critical infrastructure,etc. this could be done either  online or on a written form for those without internet access.

They would be provide with a short document briefly outlining each parties policies on each policy. Questions would then be things like "What is the Conservatives policy for the NHS a, b, c or d? Or Labour's on the environment, etc. The votor would have the information in front of them all they would have to do is read it. This way votrs with little interest in politics would at least have a basic idea of each parties policies on major issues and what they were actually voting for instead of the current situation where a large section of the electorate simply vote in line with what their parents traditionally voted for or the same as their friends or partners or base on propaganda amd misinformation pumped out by the media and online.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: I do agree with the idea of the UK implementing a 4-day work week for full-time employees. A major reason for this is that it will provide a better work-life balance for people, and if employees are less stressed, they are more likely to perform better at their job. I have also read articles online about this type of scheme being implemented in other countries, with no major negative effects caused. I think there is also a stronger will for this type of setup following COVID and remote working. I think it would be good to trial it in various sectors to see its effectiveness.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: Since a 4-day workweek for full-time professionals may significantly enhance work-life balance and lower stress, I largely support the idea. An additional day off could let people relax, spend more time with their loved ones, or work on personal objectives, which could increase their motivation and happiness at work. Furthermore, shorter workweeks have even been linked to increased productivity, however, I don't believe it would be appropriate for all kinds of jobs. For example, without additional people or planning, jobs in emergency services or healthcare may find it difficult to adapt. It is a nice idea overall but I think it needs to be adaptable to the industry.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: I agree with the statement in principle as I think we should aim to work less and get a better work-life balance. However it would have to be flexible as it may not work in some instances, and workers should have the option to work more if appropriate (but they should be paid enough for four days to reach a living wage). This ties closely with my own values as I think we should work less in a modern society. Much work is to produce goods or services we don't need, and this has a negative impact on society and of course the environment. I would go further and support Universal Basic Income, giving the opportunity for those who want to work more, and earn more, but moving away from the idea we should work and produce as a moral or practical imperative.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: I support implementing a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees. It can lead to significant improvements in work-life balance and overall mental health. Evidence from trials show that when employees work fewer hours across the week, they tend to be more focussed and productive during their time at work. This means that although working less hours, the same amount of work is getting done, if not more! A shorter week also reduces stress and burnout leading to fewer sick days and again, less stress on the NHS with job-related illnesses. Overall, the long-term benefits for both employees and employers make it worth it!

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: The UK government has complained for years that the UK is unproductive, certain politicians have claimed that this is simply because the British people are lazy and that the solution is to punish people to force them to work harder. This approach has failed over and over again whereas, if we look at other similar sized economies with greater productivity, we see better results with a more flexibility and compassion. The rigid adherence to Calvinistic work "ethic" must be abandoned for the sake of the economy. An economy that treats its workers with respect and dignity will see that positive attitude returned and productivity improved. Modern technology offers the opportunity to improve the lives of all the people of the country rather than being used to enrich a handful of business owners and private equity funds. A shorter work week with more leisure time would also stimulate the economy by giving workers more time to spend their money at businesses. A four day workweek would also save working families money that they would otherwise have to spend on childcare, allowing them to spend the money more productively elsewhere.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: It will greatly enhance British working conditions and mental health at the same time for people to work only a four day week instead of a five day one. Productivity will remain about the same, in fact it may even increase as workers strive to complete their tasks a day early with greater concentration and effort. Work expands to fit the time available. Cut down the time and the work still gets done. Also, general health will improve because of the reduction in stress a three day weekend will bring, and tourism and other local attractions will benefit from people having more time on their hands and the same wages to travel and relax and spend more time with their families and hobbies.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: I believe that studies have shown an increase in productivity where a four day week has been tried. It is surmised that office workers  may pace their work over five days and slow down on Friday, but if working four days they speed up and fit more work into a shorter time. Workers would be happier at work and stay in the same post for longer, which save the employer money on reappointment of new staff and training them. Absenteeism would fall, as would the rates of staff seeking help for stress. However it may not be suitable for all jobs. For example primary school class teachers, where consistency of staff across the week is important.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: a 4 day work week is a great idea as studies have already been conducted and shown that shorter work week increase overall productivity when compared to a 5 day week. Employees would also be generally happier as they would be able to spend more time with their family, friends and on hobbies meaning a better work life balance and a better mental health for the workers. a shorter work week would also reduce expenses for companies that need the workers physically in an office/workspace by saving money on electric bills and other similar expenses, potentially allowing the money to go into workers wages or something similar.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: If the U K was to implement a 4 day workweek for all full time employees it would go a considerable way towards people feeling that they can attain a better quality of life. More free time and more choice regarding what to use the free time for and consequently a more relaxed way of using your 3days of work as opposed to a five day week with 2 days of which can all feel as though your free time is a bit rushed.  By implementing the 4 day week it is likely that the same amount of work gets accomplished as the people working this schedule would be happier

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: I am currently working compressed hours. I do two full time weeks over 9 days. The extra day off allows me to get some jobs done, like housework, diy, shopping and still allows me to have time for hobbies, social activities and rest across the weekend. By doing the same hours, but adding parts of the extra day to the others means I can sustain a mental momentum with work - there's one less day where I have to come in and pick up where I left off, which has a time cost associated with it. It's a win/win for me and my employer, across the two weeks I have a higher mental momentum, I am less tired and a better work/life balance means I am happier in general, which reflects in the work I do.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text:
Risk of increased workload and stress: A 4 day work week basically means longer day and longer working hours that might cause fatigue, lack of contration, boredom and stress thus leading to low production of work . Employees may also feel depressed and pressured.
Inequality across the workforce: Unfair for hourly or work shifts could lead to job and income insecurity because most people are paid hourly and if the hours are reduced it simply means low wages and income
Potential impact on public service: It will lead to reduced pubvlic services e.g NHS, Local councils and policing which will lead to more deathgs due to health matters and high crime rates

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text:
presently, people are working (supposedly) a 5 day week. Have you ever tried to get anything done, lately? it is impossible. nothing works, nothing is on time, nothing is easy. can you imagine the chaos that would ensue of this were to be reduced to a four day week? I would support phasing out some jobs, such as train and tube drivers. i would welcome them being fully automated and the drivers being put on permanent zero day weeks as a means to drive productivity and efficiency.

Our country suffers from idleness. we do not work hard enough, we do not produce enough. we are being left behind by countries who only 10-15 years ago were so much poorer than us, such as Poland and Czech Republic. I am pleased for them, but we are just watching them stroll past us as we live in a country with no houses and pot-holed roads.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: During the COVID pandemic many people worked from home. Initially it appeared that productivity was not impacted but increasingly employers have endeavoured to get their workers back in the office and this has met resistance. I believe a 4-day working week will have a similar affect. Within my own organisation people requested a 4-day working week but everyone wanted either a Friday or a Monday off. This placed a strain on resources during the rest of the week and also meant you couldn't get hold of people when you needed to. Those people who worked 4 days worked longer hours but this meant they were working when other colleagues were not which also made communication difficult. Once the 4 day week is established people are very resistant to change even if it is a requirement of the organisation. Again from my own experience colleagues would not change their day off even if it had been agreed that they could be asked to do so.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: The statement above does not clarify the conditions for a 4-day mandatory workweek for all full-time employees. It is not clear what the monetary consequences would be for businesses and/or employees. Since I am not fully aware of this I can only answer for myself. I would personally enjoy a 4-day workweek even if it implied having a lower income. I think this kind of policy would only be popular if it assured that the employees income will not be reduced. Also, if this kind of change would produce an increase in job openings, then it would benefit society as a whole.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text:
A four day week could be a real asset to some people if they are able to work their hours into the 4 days instead of working 5,  If not then they may end up earning less money and the employer is willing to pay the same wage for less hours which would be at great cost to some companies. Some jobs may not afford the employer to work 4 days for example teachers have to work the 5 days of the week that the children attend school, if that was to change then it would cause massive problems for working parents.
Work productivity may increase for some people but some companies may not benefit from a break in productivity levels with a shorter working week or staff may become more stressed during the 4 days to make up for the loss of one working day.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.
rows.text: Although I could be supportive of this because it would suit me, the organisation I work for has a mix of staff who work varying hours.  Half of our workforce does 9-5 and half do shifts over a 24 hour period.  I do not see how we could make it fair to all employees.  We are a charity and funded to deliver services which have very little room in them for us to make a surplus so it would add costs and potentially mean that we are losing money on the services we run which would mean that we can no longer deliver on them.  This would leave many people in our communities without access to mental health support and put further stress on the NHS.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a 4-day workweek for all full-time employees.

Presumable the business would be picking up the cost which could have a negative effect on their sustainability and the economy. Businesses may not be able to afford to do this and it may affect their ability to operate successfully.
For things such as the NHS it could have a very detrimental effect as we already have huge waiting lists and its difficult to get a GP appointment. If staff were only working 4 days per week I can see the problems increasing

rows.proposition: The UK should sever all formal ties with the Catholic Church.

There are many different faiths practiced within the UK and they should be treated on an equal footing. The Catholic church is riddled with abuse, coverups, scandals, and controversy, not to mention the historical atrocities that have been committed in the name of the church. The Catholic church has caused so much heartache and pain to so many and continues to do so when things like child abuse are still routinely covered up. The UK needs to stand strong and allow all faiths an equal footing without allowing one faith to rule above others.

rows.proposition: The UK should sever all formal ties with the Catholic Church.
rows.text: Severing all constitutional ties between the Catholic Church and the UK would have enormous historical and cultural repercussions, risking destabilizing a centuries-old relationship that has touched numerous aspects of British life. Furthermore, any such action could seriously undermine charitable work and social welfare activity where collaboration between Church and state has traditionally been a lifeline for communities. It is also worth bearing in mind that a step towards completely cutting ties would risk offending a substantial section of the population who identify themselves as Catholic, or indeed those who appreciate the wider contribution of the Church to the social community. It is generally a good idea to have some form of formal interaction for the sake of open debate, collaboration on issues of mutual public concern, and good interfaith relations in a plural society.

rows.proposition: The UK should sever all formal ties with the Catholic Church.
rows.text: The catholic church plays a major role in ensuring children integrate with other religions in school and that they receive a very high standard of education. From what I remember as a child the church is very much involved in charitable work, in which it offers vital support to homeless and vulnerable people. It is more than just a religion it brings peace and prosperity to communities. There are millions of Catholics in the UK and it would be seen as very dangerous to cut ties to a faith that is seen as very important to a large chunk of society in the UK. Ending the ties of a big religion would be extremely divisive, especially as it has so much heritage, culture and historical significance to the British people.

rows.proposition: The UK should sever all formal ties with the Catholic Church.
rows.text: The proposal that UK should sever all formal ties with the Catholic Church is both unprecedented and unfounded.  Approximately 10% of the UK population are Catholic and while some may regard UK as a secular state, the majority of the population would still regard themselves as Christian.  Accordingly, the state severing all ties with what is the largest Christian organisation by membership worldwide would be an offensive suggestion to both Catholic and non-Catholic citizens.  While the Catholic Church has had its problems in recent years, it is also responsible for many charitable works worldwide and remains both a force for good and a positive influence in most people's lives.

rows.proposition: The UK should sever all formal ties with the Catholic Church.

I would imagine it would pain them immensely if the UK severed formal ties with the Church. The UK also has formal ties with communities such as Irish Catholics who make up a significant proportion of the UK population. It would potentially be detrimental to society if the UK cut their historic ties.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.
rows.text: To loosen the regulations on international pharmaceuticals could mean that drugs which do not pass the stringent standards in the UK could easily slip through the net. This would put people at risk. There is also the potential issue of creating a 'black market' of drugs where drugs not validated in the UK, or even illegal in the UK, could be brought into the country under the less strict regulations. This potentially puts everyone at risk. There is also the issue of the lack of medical supervision when taking these drugs. It is a very dangerous move to lessen the regulations rather than tighten them.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.
rows.text: At the heart of this issue is that of regulation.  Sadly since leaving the EU we have opened ourselves up to lower quality products such as those produced in the USA.  The current administration in the US is no way competent and I would never wish to take anything that has been approved of by Robert Kennedy.   The only reason I can see for allowing such imports would be to save money.  The only by product of this is to reduce quality. We need to maintain ahigh standard of health care and not look to cut corners in any way.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.
rows.text: Allowing a reduction in regulations could lead to less effective and less quality control on the products which are brought into the UK, as an extension of this, by allowing lower quality and tested products in, this would potentially mean UK importers and prodviders may make larger profits on a basic human right. It is unlikely medication prices will drop as a result of importing international products, there is no gain for the average person in this scenario. On a longer term view, removing legisaltion may start a slippery slope scenario, remove some regulations today and it is easy for lobby groups to make the same arguement further down the road.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.

Given the burden of cost to the NHS for medication, having foreign made similars (if cheaper) would only be a good thing. Of course, under the initial assumption. However, I would suggest some control over the ratio of imported vs UK made pharmaceuticals to ensure that we don't lose the ability and knowledge to produce them - hypotehtically if another pandemic were to arise, this would be a vital skill to retain.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.
rows.text:
The regulations in the UK are quite tight regarding regulations of controlled drugs and pharmaceuticals. It is essential to get the correct balance here - we do not want to deregulate entirely and end up with an opioid crisis like America is struggling with. However, we do need other pharmaceuticals which are available worldwide, to be available in the UK, as long as they are regulated to some degree and safe for people to take. 
A huge amount of people are suffering - cancer sufferers, people with intense pain conditions, and it is largely unnecessary when countries such as Germany, Switzerland and USA have drugs which could assist those people.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.

I have some concerns over safety, but I am also concerned that we have shortages in the UK too - and where these pharmaceuticals are known compounds, the safety aspects seem less vital. Finally, I think we might all benefit from lower priced, internationally sourced pharmaceuticals - I can see this encouraging innovation in UK industry here in an effort to produce more and remain competitive.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.

Overall I think it will be more efficient and cost effective. It will increase access to essential drugs. 
-lower prices for consumers/patients
-improve access to medication
-quicker and more efficient
No further explanation.

rows.proposition: The UK should loosen regulations on the import and distribution of international pharmaceuticals.
rows.text:
- Safety concerns: the loosen regulations could well lead to non-official drugs entering the market which cannot be identified, posing public health risks. This could also result in lack of information for people to make informed decisions on their safety.
- Ethical issues: this could lead to imports of drugs from countries with less strict regulations and reporting, for example from clinical trials. Moreover, they may follow practices which are deemed unethical in the UK.
- Control issues: resulting in a loss of control of the processes and production of these drugs, this would have an economic and human affect in terms of labour.

However if they are pharmaceuticals that are needed now but not currently produced, I would be happier to support loosening regulations as long as it was a temporary situation and monitored. I would also like to see the pharmaceutical companies making some concessions, perhaps pricewise, to recognise the UK's more accepting position. rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text:
Some people believe that incorporating creationism alongside evolution in education would acknowledge religious diversity while others fear that it could favor certain religious viewpoint over others. The UK has been a home to a wide range of beliefs including Christians, Islam, Hinduism etc. Introducing creationism raises the challenge of deciding which interpretation to present, which could lead to controversy and risk marginalizing students from other backgrounds. To maintain fairness, an inclusive approach would be necessary to ensure all perspective are represented equally.

Mandating the teaching of creationism alongside evolution could promote academic freedom by exposing students to various viewpoints on the origin of life. Some argue that presenting both allows students to critically evaluate different explanations and then form there own conclusion. Recognizing religious beliefs in education will always foster inclusivity and respect for diverse worldviews.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.

It could be argued that this takes time in the classroom away from teaching more evidence based theories which would be a better use of classroom teaching

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text: The greater the amount of information taught to school goers, the better the chance of them being able to make individual choices on the matter, as those choices are not limited by a general lack of information. In any case, who is to say that creationism must be excluded from discussion, merely because one seeks to discuss evolution instead? Education is reliant on open discussion and acceptance of differing views, with belief being of equal value to that of factual information. Open discussion of varied topics encourages differing views and acceptance of individualism. Discuss everything and believe what you want.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text: Creativity is so important for children as they grown and develop, honing in on this skill helps them break from regimented lessons and be able to have fun and be creative. It is a skill that everyone has to some extent, but it needs to be harnessed from a young age to make the most out of these skills some people don't know they have. Creativity is part of survival, it's how you evolve and how in time things get better e.g. how the telephone, public transport, technology etc... has improved, it's about people thinking creatively outside of the box.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text: Presenting creationism alongside evolutionism gives the impression that the Bibles version of the start of the world is as valid as the scientifically proven one. Creationism has been thoroughly discredited and is a ridiculous theory. It is of interest as a religious origin story - one sharing similarities with many other faiths. In the UK, public schools should be secular and not be used to indoctrinate young minds into to superstisons and legends of theists. Of course, teach children about different religions, but include all faiths, including Pastafarianism. They should be blessed to hear about the Flying Spaghetti Monster - Blessed be his noodly Appendage.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text: We do not live in an inherently religious country and there should be separation between the education system and religion, children should be taught about evolution because that is scientifically what occurred. Creationism can be taught in Religious Education if it is offered by the school, but it should not be mandated because it is factually incorrect. The purpose and importance of education is to teach children facts, not to try to indoctrinate them into a religion, if parents want to teach their children about creationism they can but it is not and should not be the responsibility of schools.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text: I strongly disagree with the idea of mandated teaching of creationism alongside evolution. Creationism comes from religious teachings that are not based in science or evidence.  Teaching creationism as though it is equally valid to evolution could be deeply damaging to many students and their ideas of scientific principles and methods. I feel that it is important to only teach students evidence. I also feel it could potentially blur the line between church and state by alienating students who are not religious. While discussion about different worldviews are acceptable in certain subjects like religious education or philosophy, science must stay grounded and teach things that are based in tested and accepted scientific theory

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text:
It is the education's system job to do just that — educate. Where possible, they must deal with facts, with science, with verifiable information. In terms of fact, there is no question that evolution trumps creationism. In fact, we have plenty of evidence to suggest that creationism *isn't* true, and should be treated as a metaphor rather than a true narrative of humanity's inception. 

Furthermore, I believe at this point, the UK is one country that should probably begin exercising seperation of the church from the state, given the evolving landscape of religion in 21st century Britain. It's a more varied and atheistic world now.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text: Whilst many people follow creationism, and believe that the universe and all that is in it initially started with a higher being that created all that has followed, this is not reliably proven with any factual evidence. However, the opposing thinking, that the evolution of the planet and its lifeforms was through biological , genetic, or natural events,  has been well documented over hundreds of years with scientific evidence. Studies such as geology and paleontology have made many discoveries that provide strong evidence of a multitude of events over billions of years to explain how the universe is in its current form

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text:
Creationism is an opinion, some argue that it creationism is a fantasy while others hold it, with zero evidence, to be the word of (a) God.
Other theories, which creationists reject out of hand such as Evolution, have scientific credibility. 
In todays fraught and binary climate science is under attack by those who argue that man rode to work on the back of dinosaurs and presumably thought thr Flintstones was a documentary. This is dangerous as, like it or not, science, for all of its past mistakes, has saved the lives and improved the lives of millions and is likely to be our real salvation this time around if we are lucky so I think it is a mistake to start teaching children this creationist stuff as if it had any credibility

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
rows.text: I do not agree that creationism should be taught alongside evolution in public schools. It is a religious belief and it is not based in scientific fact. It would confuse young children into believing it is a valid theory alongside evolution when it is a belief and not something that has been proven. Religion should not be taught as though it is science as it would confuse children who do not know any different and may lead them to believe something that isnt true. Evolution is the proven scientific fact and should be seperate from religious beliefs which should be covered in a different subject and not alongside science.

rows.proposition: The UK should mandate the teaching of creationism alongside evolution in all public schools.
