rows.proposition: The UK should increase military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion.
rows.text: The amount that the UK have invested into Ukraine following the invasion by Russia has been horrific. While you could understand the initial support to an extent, the fact that it continues 3 years later is wholly unnecessary and naive. Our country is struggling with cost of living, and the government needs to focus on assisting its own population with these struggles rather than investing in a war that doesn't benefit them. We need to invest in things like transportation, gas, homelessness and several other issues before we even think about getting involved in a war that far away from us. It all feels like performative activism from our government just to make them look good.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase military support to Ukraine in response to the 2022 Russian invasion.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: We live in a time where things cost more, and the average household is struggling to pay bills. By adding more tax, this is adding more pressure on families. Whilst I understand that adding a tax to sugary drinks makes it less appealing to buy, so it will tackle obesity, especially childhood obesity, it won't fully attack the problem. We already pay enough tax, and adding more would only upset the people due to already stretched funds being stretched further. It's not the answer to restrict people's freedom of choice. A sugary tax was added to full-fat drinks recently, and everyone went sugar-free, and it doesn't taste the same.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: I don't see the point as I'm thinking that taxing sugary drinks is supposed to stop or reduce people drinking them for health reasons?  I feel it is yet another way of the government obtaining more money out of the general population.  It didn't work with alcohol or tobacco so why will it work with taxing sugary drinks?  Will it mean that the next thing that will be taxed is 'granulated sugar'?  What about cakes and sweets and chocolate?  Are there not other important things to focus on other than yet another tax on something that the government/s can squeeze out of people?

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: Essentially I agree with a higher tax being placed on sugary drinks as this would improve public health, put less strain on the NHS all while generating extra revenue for the country - however, I would like to be shown exactly where this revenue is going to be spent but it should be spent on improving public sectors like transport for example. This is because I feel a lot of tax payer money is wasted lobbying other MP's new business ideas not improving the well being of the public which is exactly how taxpayer money should not be spent. This could improve public satisfaction and morale while also improving the nation's health and generating more revenue.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.

Nor should sugar be replaced with chemical sweeteners which is the downside of this scheme because they can cause even more damage.  I believe a lot of obesity if caused by these drinks including their low-cal/zero counterparts.  Also they are addictive and increasing their cost will help reduce consumption although of course it invariably adversely affects the poorest more than the well -off

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: I am split in opinion on this topic,as the target audience for sugary drinks are usually those who are younger (14-25) and this target audience most likely opts for sugary drinks out of taste but also due to their affordability. If taxes go up,these drinks will be purchased less,making the tax somewhat worthless. This can however be a good thing as it could somewhat force these people into drinking healthier drinks.Keeping the younger target audience in mind,it does seem less sensible to be aiming taxes for less important items ,and so i think that taxes should be put elsewhere, with a target audience that could handle the tax with no problems.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text:
There are some benefits of having some sugar in some drinks, for instance sports drinks. But in a society where a lot of people are overweight and where children have rotten teeth way too early in life, there is a need to try and get people to look at consuming healthier options, making these drinks more expensive may make people start doing this.
Governments could look at starting to ban the sale of certain drinks or make the produces pay more to sell them or have them reduce sugary content and making healthier drinks in general. Advertising could also be banned for the sale of such drinks.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text:
To put yet another tax on us, is yet another way the Government is robbing us. They use sugar as an excuse to take even more taxes from us. Increasing this tax will not stop people buying sugary drinks and it never has. They use the excuse of obesity as a reason to apply this tax but this is not the sole reason for obesity. It's jut an easy way for them to rob us yet again. 
It is also a way to rob the poor, which is what the Goverment always seem to do because buying cheap sugary drinks may be the only thing poorer families can afford.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: I strongly disagree with the idea that the UK should increase tax on sugary drinks. Raising these taxes would disproportionately impact low income families who already face significant financial pressures and often rely on affordable sugary drinks. Increasing the cost of these products does little to address the root causes of poor nutrition and punishes personal choice. People should have the freedom to decide what they consume without excessive government intervention in their diets. Furthermore the UK already has a soft drinks tax in place, which has encouraged companies to reduce sugar content in their products. Increasing the tax would likely have limited additional benefits and instead efforts should be put on education, awareness and increasing the choices of healthier alternatives.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: The UK charges so much tax, civilians have very little money left for pleasure. We already pay sugar tax, which has resulted in manufacturers rising product prices. Many products have been reformulated with sweeteners, which has spoiled the taste of many favourites, like coca cola. It is about time that prices fall. The cost of living is far too high in the UK. The poorest people in the UK are having to cut back on food to survive. Poorer people typically eat more processed foods therefore sugar tax increases would impact the poorest people in the UK. That isn't fair at all.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: Introducing higher taxes on sugary drinks will likely lead to less consumption of these beverages in the UK. This reduction in consumption could lead to many health benefits for the population, such as reduced fat, increased energy and reduced risk of heart disease. This in turn reduces strain on the NHS in future. There are also dental benefits of refraining from sugary drinks and swapping for a healthier alternatives, as sugary drinks are one of the lead causes of cavities. This has the potential to save individuals money as dental care becomes increasingly privatised in the UK. The additional money paid can be used to encourage people to seek healthier alternatives and improve investment in these markets.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text:
It has long been known that the over consumption of sugary drinks can lead to all sorts of health problems including, but not limited to, obesity and related problems, type 2 diabetes, dental problems arising from tooth decay, heart and kidney disease. Since a lot of sugary drinks are consumed by children, this stores  up potential health problems for the future of our nation's health along with dealing with current issues amongst the adult population. It is to be hoped that an increased tax levy will decrease the purchase and consumption of these drinks.
An added benefit is that money raised from this taxation can be used to deal with the increased demand for medical attention due to the disorders mentioned earlier and can be used in other areas of health as well.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: There is an argument that the UK should increase the tax on sugary drinks, and by making them more expensive, thereby deter people from drinking them.  This in turn would help tackle obesity and dental health problems.  However, it is my opinion that this would not achieve the desired impact.  There is potential for people instead to spend a greater proportion of their income on these drinks, reducing what they have left for other purchases, because for some these drinks can be both addictive and a status symbol.  Just look at the 'Prime' craze that sucked in primary school children.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: The UK already put a sugar tax in place, and most drinks were reformulated as a result to escape the tax. In the main the reformulation was achieved by lowering the amount of sugar and increasing the amount of artificial sweeteners. This means not that many soft drinks are now subject to the sugar tax, so increasing it would not increase the tax take much. There is also the question of how healthy artificial sweeteners are. The effects of sugar are widely documented due to centuries of use, where artificial sweeteners are very recent by comparison, and in the UK only in widespread use in the last 20 years or so.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: Regarding the question as to whether or not the UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks, my initial thoughts are that no it probably should not increase taxes on them. It feels to me like this is just another way of extracting cash from the general public without it being a serious argument or debate. It is all too easy to just slap a tax on things and say it's in the interest of the nation's health, but to me id it was that serious a problem they should just ban them all together. They'r playing on people's addictions to some extent.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: More people should be advised and encouraged to not drink sugary drinks, and introducing a tax would be one way of encouraging people to choose other, less sugary and potentially healthier options that are better for them. Also, it would urge people to know more about the sugar content of drinks if they know it is more money due to the sugar and so would educate more people on the issue of sugary drinks. To save money people may choose better options, simply like water that will benefit them in the long run. I think too many drinks have too high a sugar content and it should be more visible and understandable to the public on how unhealthy they may be.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text: High sugar drinks tax would reduce consumption of sugary drinks which are proven to contribute to obesity. High tax on sugary drinks would also promote parents to purchase low sugar beverages or alternatives for children less consumption of sugary drinks in kids means less dental issues such as tooth decay, gum disease etc will develop. For both points the effect would be greater in low income areas where price of goods is a main decision making factor. In high income areas the effect of such a policy would be less but still would have a measurable impact on the population

rows.proposition: The UK should increase taxes on sugary drinks.
rows.text:
Improving health is a major target in the United Kingdom, and high sugar levels in food leads to obesity and weight gain. Having a sugar tax on unhealthier foods makes the consumer less likely to purchase them, if better alternatives that are both tasty and affordable are also put forward.


Typically people will purchase food if it tastes good, and is affordable. Therefore it is not enough to add a sugar tax to unhealthy food, but to provide consumers with a healthy, but crucially cheaper alternative to purchase instead. Otherwise the sugar tax may not be effective at the desired goal.

Additionally, high sugar consumption is correlated to obesity levels, and being overweight is a high risk factor with a myriad of other health conditions, meaning that lowering sugar consumption may reduce other health conditions, reduce weight gain and improve health outcomes overall.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text: Raising tax for oil companies will as a result raise the prices of oil for transport etc. Oil companies should pay higher taxes for the damaging effects they have regarding climate change. We as a society need to shift towards renewable energies, with higher cost it would encourage oil companies to look at other fuel methods that would potentially be better value whilst being environmentally friendly. As a society we also rely too much on cars, many people can walk/cycle/use public transport but rather choose to own their own car to drive a 5 minutes journey instead of a 15 minutes cycle for example. Most people are within a walkable distance and it's also good for their health to be more active. Raising the oil prices may encourage people to look at other environmentally friendly methods of transport.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text: I strongly agree that the UK should raise taxes for oil companies. In recent years, these companies have reported record-breaking profits, often during times when ordinary consumers have struggled with rising energy bills and a cost of living crosis. It is only fair that corporations benefiting enormously from essential resources contribute more to society, rather than allow the financial burden to fall on the middle class. By raising these taxes, the government could raise substantial revenue which could be used to fund the transition to renewable energy and support both environmental goals and long-term energy security. This would promote a more equitable tax system.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text: Oil companies are responsible for a huge amout of damage to the environment and should not be allowed to continue putting massive profits ahead of the health of the natural world. Oil causes pollution at every stage, from it's extraction to its refining, transport and it's end uses as fuel and plastics. It is vulgar that so many shareholders become rich from being part of something so destructive, and while it is profitable to do so there is no incentive to stop, so the only way to hinder them is by taxing them util it is no longer worth while. Wars are fought over oil and lives are lost, it is unacceptable.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text:
Oil is not a renewable energy and is a limited resource. We need to invest more in renewable sustainable, clean energy. By taxing fossil fuels and not taking renewable energy then the government will encourage companies to invest in renewable energy as it will be more profitable for companies.  Taxes on fossil fuels can be specifically used for investment in renewable energy sources. 

Oil companies are responsible, either directly or indirectly for pollution and so it is morally right that they should pay taxes (as well as fines for direct pollution) to cover the cost of clean up and to fund anti-pollution initiatives.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.

They absolutely should pay far more taxes and contribute some of the highest amount of money among all industries worldwide into fixing the problems. If they aren't going to do that of their own free will then they must be forced to pay legally in this kind of way in order to help the planet.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text: Oil companies are hugely to blame for the climate crisis we are experiencing. The greed of those with oil is destroying our climate. The transportation, extraction, and burning of oil contribute massively to emissions. Taxes on oil companies could be used to foster a greener economy. We are not investing enough money and resources into preserving and helping our suffering environment. The tax increase would help move the nation in the right direction and alleviate our reliance on fossil fuels. Higher taxes could be reinvested in the R&D of alternative energy sources. We cannot continue to rely on oil and put money in the pockets of those with huge oil reserves.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text:
In truth, I do not understand much about how the oil companies work.  Companies should, however, operate in an environmentally friendly way as to not worsen the effects of climate change.

Any increase in costs to the oil companies will ultimately result in increase in prices to the consumer.  There have been talks, I think, about penalising companies for not following environmentally friendly standards, but again, these costs will result in increased prices to the consumers.  Increase in taxes may also demotivate companies to find better ways of production and process, and incentives may be another option.  If so, let's see whether or not decrease in price will be passed on to the consumers.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text: There is a strong argument for raising taxes on oil companies. They are making record profits (at the same time as people's energy bills continue to skyrocket) and damaging the environment with very little regard for reinvesting the profits into more climate friendly initiatives and using cleaner technologies. However, they are heavily invested in pension schemes, so the worry is that by increasing taxes they will negatively impact the pensioners. Yet, like many other businesses they should be forced to literally 'clean up their act' or face fines at the very least. There is also the issue that they may stop investing in the UK and jobs would be lost.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text: Oil extraction and consumption has a naturally very negative impact on the environment. We can raise taxes on oil companies, this might in turn encourage more investment into renewable and green energy sources. At the same time, the extra funding raised through taxation could be used to fund vital services e.g. the NHS, public transport, education, or defence. Having said that, given our capitalist economy model, rising taxes too high can have a negative impact - if taxation is unfavourable in the UK, companies might look to move businesses elsewhere out of the country and as a result take away jobs and reduce the overall amount of tax they pay. At the same time , there's nothing to stop oil companies passing on the cost to the consumer which has a negative impact on normal people.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.

Mt concerns would be if this had a knock on effect and caused job losses. These companies would want to protect their bottom line and these cost would have to be saved elsewhere either on staffing levels or on employees salaries which may cause hardship for some.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.

rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.
rows.text: Raising taxes on oil companies could make them unsure about investing in the UK because constant tax changes create uncertainty. This could be especially harmful for the smaller companies as they don't have the same large resources as the bigger firms. Higher taxes might also result in fewer jobs in the oil industry, which supports thousands of workers and businesses. On top of that, companies may pass these extra costs onto their consumers, leading to higher energy prices and a poorer country overall having to combat those challenges. rows.proposition: The UK should raise taxes for oil companies.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the production and distribution of pornography.

Risking the coercion of trafficking and thirdly, unchecked access to degrading or violent content can distort attitudes towards sex and relationships, especially among young people.  We need to make it clear so people by making clearer standards and oversight, the UK can reduce harm, promote consent and ensure the business operates with integrity.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text: It is inarguable that Europe currently faces the most geopolitically adversity it has faced since World War II. The Russian invasion of Ukraine, the volatility in the Middle East (Israel-Hamas and Israel-Iran wars), and the unpredictability of the USA's Trump administration and their overtures of acquiring Canada, Greenland, and Panama - these are all emergent crises that demand the attention of European military/peacekeeping resources. I agree that the UK should contribute as much as possible to the remilitarisation of Europe, for our own security, for wider European security, and as a show of unity and collaboration with Europe which is sorely needed after the diplomatic disaster of Brexit. However, to truly determine whether I agree with the statement, I would need to know exactly how military spending would be increased, where the money would come from, etc.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text: In order to improve its position within NATO, I think the UK should raise its military budget, although this increase should be moderate rather than excessive. A moderate increase in financing will promote collective defense and improve international security cooperation while assisting the UK in fulfilling its NATO obligations and preserving its reputation with its partners. With this strategy, the UK can contribute significantly to international security without having to make significant sacrifices to vital public services. Spending too much on the military could take funds away from other sectors like healthcare and education, which are essential to the welfare of the society. The UK maintains its strength both domestically and globally by striking a balance between military spending and social goals, preserving its power without sacrificing the wellbeing of the society.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text:
The UK is in a bad way economically. Spending more on defence when there is no credible threat to this country is absurd. We need to address the real issues facing the country, which are the economy and mass immigration. That requires spending on those areas and easing the economic problems of the suffering British people.

The idea that Russia is intent on marching West and occupying all Europe is sheer nonsense. It is a lie, designed to frighten easily-fooled people. Russia simply does not have the will, money or the military strength to do that.

Being aggressive and pushing for a war with a country with a vastly superior nuclear capacity is madness.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.

We may not be involved in the Israel-Iran conflict but there are a lot of Jews living in the UK - so not surprising that many think the UK support Israel.
We are capable to increasing military spending to ensure that countries who are threatening us (Russia keeps sending ships and planes to infiltrate our space) - should think twice before acting upon their threats.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text:
While the world is suffering from the effects of global climate change, which will likely lead to mass migration over time to more temperate areas, leaders of some countries are waging wars on their own borders for more political reasons.  This unprovoked conflict needs a united peace keeping force, armed and funded by the majority of countries on the borders of the conflict, to allow countries to intervene without directly joining the war and declaring a side.
Conflicts such as the invasion of Ukraine and Gaza need, for humanitarian reasons if nothing else, to be brought to a close and their people allowed to rebuild their shattered country.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.

- the world is not a safe place. There is an aggressive Russia and Chinese could potentially invade Taiwan. If the spending is increased, it allowed the UK to be able to show its strength and deter any countries whom think they can intimidate the UK. 
- The UK can help in the effort to ward of Russian aggression, they can use the money to help Ukraine. The money could use the spending to help in Europe and potentially if Taiwan is attacked by China.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.

Equally there is a danger in solely relying on the US for hardware and defence systems when a better model is to develop our own capabilities and ensure a more independent and robust defence policy.

NATO helps in peace keeping and treats all NATO members fairly.  We need NATO for many other reasons too and one way this can be done effectively is by increasing our military spending to strengthen NATO's vital and beneficial role for all of us.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text: Although the UK is in a cost of living crisis and many departments, such as education, health and social care and police are seriously underfunded, we do need to ensure that the UK is protected and to ensure that we can protect other countries that are under threat. We need to protect democracy across the globe and being part of NATO is crucial in order to do this. As the US are reducing their contribution and may even leave NATO altogether, this threatens security across the world. Due to under-funding by successive governments, the UK needs to increase spending for it's military to ensure that we can protect ourselves and others with updated equipment so that our arm forces have what they need to work effectively and efficiently.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text:

Increased spending will also unite us with our NATO allies showing them that the UK is fully committed to the alliance.
The increase in spending will allow the UK to invest in the latest technologies and advanced weaponry needed in this day and age to keep up with the ever changing evolution of weaponry which would be needed if we ever had to go to war. We also need the extra money to train all NATO nations to have a united front and to work together seamlessly should we be required to do so.

rows.proposition: The UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.
rows.text: I agree that the UK should increase its military spending to strengthen its role within NATO.  By doing so, the UK demonstrates its commitment to the alliance and builds greater trust with its international partners. Meeting the NATO target of spending at least 2% of GDP on defense not only fulfills a shared responsibility but also shows leadership within the alliance. In today's world, where geopolitical tensions are rising and global security is becoming more unpredictable, having a strong and modern military force is essential. It allow the UK to protect its national interests more effectively and contribute meaningfully to regional and global stability.

Just look at Ukraine. This is an example of what can happen to a country that is not protected and part of this allied force. Russia do not want Ukraine nor any of it's neighbouring  countries to be members as it wants to increase it's world power by moving back to its USSR status. Ukraine is just the start. NATO is a huge deterrent to countries thinking they can march in and take over. 
Also it is incredibly important that the USA does not think it's the only real power in this alliance. They must be kept on board and feel like all the other countries and doing whatever is possible to be as military capable as possible, not simply relying on the USA.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly increase its military spending.

In my opinion therefore we do really need to increase our defence spending, and quickly as events seem to be moving at a rapid pace. This has also been highlighted with the escalation of actions in the Middle East, between Israel and Iran.

Being prepared for any sort of war is essential, the technology we use today is expensive and we need to keep up with the preparedness of other countries that are ready to go to war. If we don't we may be on the backfoot of anything that may happen here, and it could be devastating for all.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly increase its military spending.

I fear that without increasing our military spending and capacity we may one day find ourselves aiding another country to the detriment of our own defences - especially if the aggressor in that conflict then views us as another potential target or enemy for giving our support against them. It would be foolish to underestimate the threat of this scenario.

rows.text: Due to the present climate of conflict in the world (such as the situation in Israel) increasing military spending enables the currently to effectively defend itself.  Furthermore, a increase in military spending will also allow the armed forces to be modernized which will be helpful if war would occur.  It is also worth bearing in mind that an increase in military spending will create job opportunities, which will help the economy.  And finally, enhancing military funding will better equip the armed forced to provide humanitarian aid - ensuring that the UK military is recognized for providing international support to countries in times of need.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly increase its military spending.
rows.text: I think defense spending takes too much of money. This is quite unnecessary because there are more important causes in the country that need to be addressed such as cost of living, economy etc. This is wasteful expenditure as all it does is result in killing of people. I think UK should adopt a neutral stance and not get involved in other countries' affairs. It is unfair to spend this amount of money on other countries when our own country has so many unmet needs such as long waiting lists at the NHS hospitals and people not being able to afford basic necessities. There is child poverty that could do with this money.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly increase its military spending.
rows.text:
Do not agree with this since do not feel that there is a real need to increase military spending as the current level is more than adequate.
Feel that we as a country spend already too much on defence and an increase should not be warranted.
There are better things to spend money on than in defence- areas such as housing, education, social services and of course the NHS should have a higher priority. These are areas that I feel the UK public would benefit more if less was spent on defence.
Do not see the need to increase spending since current defence id ok.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly increase its military spending.
rows.text: I partially agree that the UK should spend a lot more on the military. On the one hand, ensuring long-term security and safeguarding the nation against escalating international threats require a more robust national defense. Additionally, it helps the UK fulfill its international commitments, including those within NATO, and support its allies, which contributes to the stability of the world. However, a large increase in the military budget would divert funds from social services, healthcare, and education - all of which are critical to the welfare of the country. Furthermore, increased military spending could fuel tensions between nations and lead to a global arms race, undermining diplomatic and peace initiatives. Therefore, while some increase in military investment may be necessary, it should be balanced carefully with other national priorities.

rows.proposition: The UK should significantly increase its military spending.
rows.text: We need to be able to defend ourselves against other countries and increasing our defence spending is required to do this. We need to be ready to defend ourselves and therefore need to ensure we have spent enough before anything happens, so we are ready to defend ourselves at short notice at any given time. The world is becoming increasingly more hostile with more wars between countries. Some countries are supporting others who are at war, for example the UK have provided funding to Ukraine to help them defend themselves from Russia. Russia could choose to turn on us for this although with America as our ally this is unlikely. We are no longer a part of Europe which in my eyes is not a good thing. I would prefer we were reunited with Europe again. I personally did not agree with Brexit as we could be more vulnerable now.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: Brexit has shown that even outside of the EU the UK is beholden to it's guidelines. Global trade and economic activity requires cooperation on on regulations and customs. This is the same for major EU trade partner, not just the UK. Being outside of the EU means we have to follow these rules without any ability to change or influence them. Furthermore, re-joining the EU would remove tariffs and other trade barrier, encouraging economic growth and international organisation to build their UK presence. Being one of the biggest and more influential countries in the EU would also benefits the UK's power projection and ability to influence other EU nations.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: When the UK voted to leave the EU, many people genuinely believed that we would, as a nation, be better off financially (more money for the NHS, for example) and that the financial shock of leaving would be quickly overcome.  In fact, we are worse off, individually and as a nation and there is no immediate prospect of things improving.  Good trade deals have been slow to come and those that have been achieved still haven't come close to replace the business lost with the EU.  In a globalised world we are now something of an anachronism. Also, with war on the horizon, we need to keep our friends close and our relationships strong.  Geographical commonsense says we belong with Europe, because our interests align.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text:
We should definitely re join the European Union. We would have better trade with Europe, making our food and other products cheaper and we would all be able to support one another. The EU has always been a major trading partner and it would be smoother and more economically viable if we were a part of the EU again.

We would be more secure and safe if we were part of the EU again. We are stronger together. We have a stronger voice if we act as one. The government should do another referendum with the correct information this time.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: There are many positives to rejoining the EU. Access to the single market benefits UK businesses, with wider potential markets and fewer tariffs. Many small businesses in particular have suffered as a result of Brexit. It also enhances our security, due to the shared defence capabilities associated with membership of the EU. This includes cybersecurity and general global instability. Freedom of movement for the workforce benefits UK employers' ability to recruit skilled workers and enables citizens to more easily travel and work abroad. Eu membership also facilitates greater collaboration in education, arts, etc giving greater opportunities to young people in particular.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: The EU definitely isn't perfect but the advantages of being a member greatly outweigh the disadvantages. The UK's economy is not performing as well as it could right now, and while this is true of many economies, having access to the EU's single market and combined negotiating power would definitely improve performance. Additionally, the ability to live, work, and travel freely throughout the EU is something I would personally love to have, and I am sure this is true of many others. The one thing we would have to consider is the terms of any deal to rejoin, the deal we had before we left was incredibly advantageous, I am unsure as to how generous any new deal would be.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: In the past the UK was never part of the European Union. People were told that we would be 'better off' joining it, and so the majority voted to join it. After this, some things were a lot better, but people also had to do what the EU government wanted them to do. In some aspects, it was a good thing, but also it cost our country a lot of money to join the EU and stay a member of it. Some of our 'freedom' was removed and a lot of people felt we should join Brexit (me included). If we can't govern our own rules, then we deserve what we get. Why should it take many countries to have the majority vote on if something is right or wrong? Doesn't common sence come into it any more?

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.

To protect our resilient in troubled times we need to be as self sufficient as possible, what would happen in a future crisis where we could not trade with others? millions could suffer. Plus it seems funny how nobody seems to see anything wrong in importing products and food we could have supplied in our own country. The environment is being destroyed by this ridiculous system.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: I think it is important to respect the majority vote of the general public. In my opinion you cannot ask a nation to vote on something and if they did not vote for what you wanted, you try and overrule it. They voted to leave and this should be respected as such. Also, UK being out of the EU means we have greater control over several elements of trade, immigration, borders and regulations. If we joined again, this would be at risk and we may risk going backwards in terms of the amount of control the UK has over things.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: I think that the UK should definitely rejoin the EU. it was something to be very proud of. the freedom of movement is what I found most amazing about it. and now that there is a threat that this will be taken away it makes me angry. I also understand that better trade deals and defence will be much better in the EU. I know that some people dont believe this but it was clear and is clear now that we are being excluded from some things including grants and funding from the EU. As stated mainly the reason for me is the fact that freedom of movement has been taken away from me.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.

All of the promises made regarding illegal immigration seem to not have come to fruition, as there are still many people trying to enter the country. It seems that the UK always sends money to other countries so it wouldn't make much of a difference to the waybill as we would probably contribute just as much as we do now.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text:
The European union is good for trade as it is one of the largest trading places in the world, there is much to be said about being part of that and being a trading partner with them, also people are free to move in an area where they can live and trade freely with other countries. 
It is good to feel part of the continent that we are part of and not adrift of it, being part of the European Union can offer more protection in terms of threats from other countries. But as a whole I believe that it is good for trade to avoid tariffs on goods and things like that.
However people will feel that we have already voted not long ago to be not part of it which adds a whole different dimension to the argument, so that it why I am not at all fully in agreement with rejoining.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.

I do believe that people were getting annoyed with the illegal immigration and the constant flow or boats coming to our shores that this could fix it and that is why they voted to leave. rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.

by joining eu, uk can reap benefits of trade which is proving quite costly at the moment. people will be able to live a slightly better life which is impacted by tough economy due to exit from eu. currently people are paying high prices for almost everything including energy and other daily use commodities. people pay huge amount of money in terms of taxes on product imported from eu hence cost of living is impacted negatively for people of Uk.
people will also feel a sence of unity by joining eu and would be able to fly freely within eu. people i personally know had homes across eu but would not want to go there and were feeling a divide because of brexit so joing back eu will give them a sense of family again society will be living peacefully and happily.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.

rows.proposition: The UK should seek to rejoin the European Union.
rows.text: I don't think the UK should try to re-join the EU. Now that Brexit has happened, it makes more sense to focus on rebuilding independence rather than going back under EU control with tighter conditions. Re-joining would almost certainly come with fewer privileges than before, and the UK might lose control over key areas like immigration, regulation, and trade policies. The EU has also been criticised for being overly bureaucratic and slow to respond to crisis, which doesn't suit the UKs need for agility. Instead, we should push for better global trade agreements, strengthen the economy, and invest in national resilience rather than re entering a system we already voted to leave.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: Whilst it is true that some minors will be affected by watching violent video games and go on to perpetuate violence, the instances of this are rare and could be compared to watching real live footage of war zones and injury. The majority of minors accessing these games are either getting them from friends, who could be older, or using fake birth date information on gaming platforms to access them. In addition, parents need to be accountable for what their children are accessing - leaving this to the government to police is ineffectve. They should be setting parental controls on their childrens devices.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: Young people can be very impressionable and playing violent video games probably doesn't help with their development. If a younger person sees something violent in a video game, they may feel the need to react to it in some way and may want to reenact it in real life. However I also believe there should be an element of responsibility from the parents to ensure they are on top of what their child is playing and that they are ensuring their safety. There should be regulations and shops need to adhere to these, but the parent should definitely also take responsibility for this.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.

The themes in these games are often inappropriate for developing minds. Stricter laws would make it easier for parents to control what their children are exposed to. Retailers and manufacturers would have to be more responsible for the content of the video games that they make and sell. Having regulations would bring video games into line with television and films which have age guidance

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: On the surface of it, of course we should ensure that under-18s are protected from unsuitable things, whether that is games, media or anything else. However, I don't know how this could be achieved in this day and age, when most are digitally downloaded I think. Also, I don't think that this is our biggest problem. Our biggest problem in my opinion is not games, it is hardcore pornography and also people like Andrew Tate et al. Outside of the internet, in real life, our biggest barriers to law and order , and biggest risks to young people, is that our social contracts have been broken, in my opinion: It has been building up for a while but I think lockdown was the ultimate last straw. Since then, it seems ok to do whatever you like and face no consequences, and maybe the young people are thinking 'why shouldn't I?' So knife crime, shoplifting, it's a free-for-all.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: There is evidence based research to indicate that violence based video games can promote violent behaviour in minors as it enables them to out the violence in the game and can also deteriorate their mental health. This would have long lasting negative consequences for the minor as they progress into their adult life. On the other hand, children in the 90's for example grew up with fairly violent cartoons such as Tom and Jerry where the characters poison, skin and cook each other, yet this did not appear to incite violence. It could also be that the reason we think that shows like Tom and Jerry did not promote violence was that there is not much research on this.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: Minors' brains are not fully developed yet. I have confidence that most of the youth understand that the video games they're playing are not real and are fictional, however, the overuse of violent video games can normalise the behaviour and situations to young people, and make it seem like they are integral to everyday life. Given that your frontal lobe isn't fully developed until you are in your mid-twenties, the exposure to violence can impact psychological development as a teenager grows up and develops into a young adult. This can cause aggression, and shorter attention spans, things that will impact real life situations and relationships.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: I don't think, though I may be incorrect, that there is a link between violent video games and real-life violence; therefore, I don't think there is a need for stricter regulations. Furthermore, it should be the responsibility of parents and schools to make sure their children aren't violent, not the responsibility of video game companies. Children should be educated correctly, in terms of social behaviour, and should be taken care of properly. Also, children can access all sorts of violent material on the internet, and there is no good way to regulate this. If a minor wants to engage with violent content, there is no good way to stop this.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: This is a topic which I'm not too fussed about. I can't see how playing video games is going to affect a minor to behave in an irresponsible way. I watch lots of horror films when growing up and it certainly didn't affect me in my everyday life. These are just games which minors enjoy. At least it keeps them off the streets and getting up to mischief. I think there are far more pressing matters in the world. Also, I thought there were already restrictions on certain games, like there are with films at the cinema. Minors would get hold of them anyway, let's face it!

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: My initial thought is that it is a waste of time and resources (as stricter regulation needs more money) for something that is not a huge issue in the grand scheme of our current society - I would much rather see added investment to increase social housing or funding the NHS for example. I also believe that in our current regulations, kids cannot buy the video games themselves so a parent or older sibling is doing it for them, which would likely continue even with stricter regulations - they will just find new workarounds and it will be a losing battle. My final point is that there isn't any concrete evidence of increased violence in minors by playing violent video games, so why try and fix an issue that may not be that big.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text: In my opinion, the law currently strikes a good balance between safeguarding minors and personal freedom. Whilst it's clear that extremely young children should not be exposed to violence in general, there is no evidence to show that violent video games cause societal harm. In fact, a number of studies have shown the opposite when this was first raised as a discussion point in the media in the early to mid 2000s. In particular, there is no correlation with consumption of violent media, be that films, tv or games and the level of violence. The supposed link appears to stem from the media being sensationalist. Finally, there is a general principle that government should interfere as little as possible. As there is no evidence that regulations should be made stricter, they shouldn't be.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement stricter regulations on the sale of violent video games to minors.
rows.text:
The primary responsibility f guiding and monitoring media consuption by children, espeially video games, should lie squarely on parents or gurdians. This is not a responsibility of the government and families should understand what is or is not appropriate for their children. The UK has already enforced a robust age rating system which provides clear guidance for video game consumers hence leaving the rest for the parents and gurdians.
Research on the direct causial link of violent video games and real life violence is complex and non-conclusive.Imposing stricterregulations based on a link which is still being debated might be an overreach without definitive proof.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: this is a very good idea, as long as the amount given is only given once, i.e. if the money goes they cannot have any more. People would be able to budget better and at least would know what they have each month. If it replaced other benefits it could potentially mean a great saving to the country, which is something required at the moment. The world is changing and more and more costs are in every country's sights. The amount would have to be carefully worked out, but should ensure the rent can be paid and household expenses met.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: The universal basic income would mean that everybody starts on a level playing field, with nobody better off than anybody else. Of course, with those who have inherited incomes it could be problematic - perhaps the concept could be means-tested so that the basic income really is for everybody. If wealth could not be inherited over a certain sum, it would make society more equal, with the monies removed from the very rich to distribute to everyone. This idea would be contested by those who would, like some people say in Trump's America, 'It's communism! but the success of the Singapore model which works in a capitalist country demonstrates that the policy is, and can be, universal for all economic models.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: Implementing the universal basic income in the UK could  boost  social peace and economic steadiness. By giving every adult a set of monthly sum, the plan promises to cut poverty  shrinking the gap between rich and poor, particularly for low paid people. That regular income helps families buy food , pay rent and cover transport, giving them room to breathe. This also has been found out to boost their mental and physical health. Swapping the tangled web of means-tested benefit for one no-questions-asked payment would also shave bureaucracy and strip away the stigma that can shadow needy claimants. Freedom to study, retrain, or look after children without panicking about next weeks bill opens up paths people otherwise feel forced to skip. Of course, any Universal basic income must be funded wisely and adjusted over time, but with that care it could move the UK toward a fairer, kinder society.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text:
The state the UK is in right now is morally reprehensible, there are thousands and thousands of children who go hungry every day while there are still millionaires and billionaires who make more money than a reasonable person could ever spend in their lives. 

UBI has been shown by many countries to be an effective remedy to unemployment, poverty, and healthcare. so many issues span from people being poor and therefore, becoming a burden on a nation's finances. these issues could be prevented if every person received a basic security payment to ensure they can afford food, heating and medicine.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: I am of the opinion that everybody should be receipt of a basic universal income regardless of their ability to work or contribute to society.There are many people in the UK not just through laziness but other factors like disability and inadequate job opportunities or if you have reached an age in life where you find it increasingly difficult to support yourself thus being able to receive a living wage would make a huge difference in these peoples lives. In the UK the emphasis is if you are of a certain age then you must be able to work and clearly the figures show this is not true and people receiving a basic universal income are only merely just getting by in life.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: If everyone had the right to a basic salary then it would ensure that everyone would have the ability to buy necessities like food or medication. It would also mean that everyone is paid a basic income for work that they do and prevent people from claiming benefits without good reason. However, it could also be misused by people who don't want to work as they would know that they have a basic salary without doing anything to be entitled to it. Therefore, while I believe that overall a universal basic income would be a positive thing, it would need guidelines to prevent it from being abused.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: I feel today there is a higher unemployment rate and many younger people are struggling due to covid and adapting to so called normal life. I think that ensuring all people have sufficient income to be able to not end up homeless is important. This is pretty simplistic view and I am sure this type of social engineering can lead to exploitation of the social welfare grants. I am not sure this type of system leads to more young entrepreneurs who take risks and generate income. I believe sometimes there is too much state interference. Also this type of assistance offered is costly and lead to tax hikes or increased borrowing by the government which would lead to increase in debt repayments.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: i believe on the face of it it is a good idea and people are struggling to pay for daily essentials such as food and electric. but also should everyone receive the same, should people that are high earners receive the same amount as people who are on minimum wage, is that fair? i believe that on the surface is sounds like a good idea but the cost would be alot for the country and the money would have to come from somewhere and would be taken from other services. the idea sounds good but im not sure it would work.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text:
I agree in principle that the UK should have a basic wage. This stops workers from being extorted and being taken advantage of. However this basic wage should not be so high that it starts to affect the free market economy. For example, if a coffee shop chain has to pay large amounts to it's staff, the cost of it's goods and services will have to rise, resulting in less people spending with them. Ultimately the company closes or moves to another country. Jobs are lost and the economy suffers.
Also I feel strongly that someone working in Tesco, beeping orders at a checkout should be paid significantly less than say, a health care assistant on a busy hospital ward and has much more responsibility. At the moment, people are leaving these types of NHS jobs because they are too stressful and demanding. Instead they can be paid the same hourly rate of pay in a supermarket. This is not right.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: A universal basic income for all citizens has both negative and positive benefits. The main benefits of this would be a huge reduction in poverty, which in turn would increase the general health of the population. It would ensure that every citizen would receive a fixed income regardless of their employment status, With this it would provide a safety net. I do think that potentially it could close the gap between the highest and lowest earners. However, i think that the negatives of this system is cost. If the UK was to provide a universal income, it would require significantly more government funding, potentially a lot more than the government has available. So this could in turn lead to a higher tax burden on working individuals

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: There are several countries from my understanding that have implemented UBI and the results were positive. I believe that if UBI was to be implemented, there would have to be caution exercised, with the main focus being getting people into jobs. In theory, if people had the money to sufficiently support themselves, healthcare systems would ideally struggle less, as there will be a healthier population. In the same vein, children would live more enriching, safer and healthier lives as the parents would not have to find money. Allowing people the time and freedom to search for a job as well, without worrying about money, would in turn boost the economy. It could ultimately be seen as an investment in the future.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: Every Society is flourishung at the expences of the poorest people and we need to start again and rethink what is really important in our life, which is not money. The selfishness of the few has always deteriorated and ruined the lives of innocent people that are played like pawns, used and thrown away, considered more like numbers than human beings. We should aboliosh the concept of money and found our existence in acts that will benefit each other, an exchage that will bring back dignity and focuse more on the role of each citizen as a example of inspiration for all.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: Sadly there are too many lazy people in society who have no motivation or incentive to work hard, earn an honest wage, pay taxes and contribute to the good of our society. A universal income will further encourage more people to opt out of employment, which will have a detrimental effect on many employment sectors who are already struggling to fill vacant posts. It is also unfair on hard working tax payers who will have to cover the cost of this income through their own hard work. Instead, people should be challenged and encourage to work hard, earn a wage, pay taxes and make a positive contribution to society. However, there are genuine people who are unable to work due to health issues and a robust and fair benefit system should be in place to support their financial needs.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text:
I have heard this idea mooted but to be honest it doesn't feel real or realistic, certainly not in the UK context. Having said that, I haven't looked into the arguments for it. I have perhaps heard that it's been adopted somewhere in Scandinavia but I'm reluctant to assume that's automatically a good thing. For a start, we are culturally very different to everywhere in Scandinavia. It feels like it takes the idea of the welfare state to an unnatural conclusion.

We also live in capitalist societies, for better or worse, and I fail to see how this would solve any of the problems associated with that. Surely, we'd just arrive at inflated prices and wages by another route so that people felt their education, work skills and effort were being fairly rewarded. To me, it just feels like a fancy way of dressing up benefits and on one level, I do object to unearned money being called 'income'.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text:
I am swaying towards no for this statement. I understand it would give people a basic standard of living but I do genuinely believe that some would use this as a reason to never find genuine work, pay taxes and contribute to the economy. 

How would the Govt fund this idea when they are already struggling to balance the books? I think it would be better to introduce more apprenticeship type roles where young people would be paid to learn a skill thus encouraging them to work, contribute to the economy and have a sense of purpose. We need to focus on upskilling our population so we are less dependent on other countries for skilled labour.

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text: The concept of UBI is similar to the concept of communism.  Everybody treated in the same way.  Except that it never works that way in communist countries and UBI would not create equality here either.  The rich would get the money but it would be irrelevant, the poor would get it but it wouldn't be enough to make their lives good.  Therefore it is hard to understand why UBI would be seen to be a good thing.  It is just benefits as they are now but dressed up in wording to make it sound like something more.  UBI experiments carried out around the world have failed to prove that it is a good, workable idea

rows.proposition: The UK should implement a universal basic income for all citizens.
rows.text:
I believe in this world people should be able to earn what they want for the jobs they do and the hard work they put in. Different levels of jobs demand different levels of hard work, commitment and sacrifice from humans so different level of pay should be rewarded to fall in line with their commitment.
If everyone was on the same basic pay the world would slowly fall apart and not cope as I don’t think people would have disposable income to waste or to utilise to keep the economy going like it is in today’s world at all.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: I absolutely think the monarchy could be abolished. I have never understood why one group of people should be living in the way that the monarchy does. To me it's a complete mystery. They are people, part of the human race, just as I am. How can it be right that they to have so much, far, far more than they could ever need, when there are so many who suffer daily because they do not have enough, children going to bed hungry, others freezing in their own homes, some without even a home. If that wealth was shared the country would be a much nicer place for many.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: The monarchy continues to become outdated the farther we head into the century, especially when considering how much money from the taxpayer is used for lavish celebrations for coronations and weddings or renovating the palace. They may draw in tourism, but they are still a family who, to me, do not earn that kind of power — a power that seems quite underused to begin with when it comes to laws, etc — unlike democratically-elected leaders such as a Prime Minister or President. However, it is not as simple as one would hope, as the assets would more than likely going to the family rather than the state.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text:
Tradition in the UK is something to be proud of, however the Royals have annoyed me over the recent years as they bring embarrassment to the country with their unscrupulous antics.
The money allocated to the Royals is disgusting, especially travel, property, lifestyle and education. Our country needs attention and money directing towards the NHS not the Royal family.
I believe the government should cut the Royal families allowance and use it to improve our NHS and other needy causes. The Royals do not need all the properties and land that they own, they are parasites.
I do hope that one day soon the government will take action via a public vote, I would vote to abolish the Royal family.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: Constitutionally the UK has a unique system where the Crown plays a key roll in the stability of the country, it is so embedded in Law that it makes it near impossible to destabilise the country either through a Military coup or any other illegal means.  Internally this makes the UK an attractive country to invest in long term which benefits UK subjects.  The Monarchy promotes UK Business interests, high lightening what the UK as to offer the World. This brings in billions of pounds in to the Country. The Monarchy when associated with any charity, highlights that charity in ways most individuals are unable to do both domestically and internationally, therefore boosting those charities in to the spot light which in turn provides revenue.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text:
Elected leaders only tend to appeal to their supporters by virtue of being elected on a partisan basis, and so struggle to bridge the divide and provide leadership in moments where someone is needed to represent the nation as a whole to express emotions. 

For example, in the event of a major flood that killed a large number of citizens, the elected leadership can turn up and say some nice words but in partisan times those words are not going to be of much comfort for supporters of the other political parties that consider the elected leader responsible for not better handling the floods. Meanwhile a non-partisan monarch is able to offer words of comfort that don't attract the same "this is your fault" reaction.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: I think UK should keep its monarchy. There are several reasons. The first one is UK's monarchy is very unique, with its long heritage. Furthermore, UK monarchy, due to its long standing history and uniqueness, is much appreciated by other countries and can boost tourism in UK. For instance, anything related with UK monarchy can be used to increase the number of tourists visiting UK. While there is cost associated with maintaining UK monarchy, the benefit outweighs the cost. It must be noted that the benefit of maintaining UK monarchy does not necessarily always correlate with money.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

Some people might not like them but the members of the royal family have just grown up to act the way they do (posh) because that is what they were taught from birth and they do not know any differant. People should appreciate the royal family because they are not a bad family, they are a good family and people love reading about them.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

Other countries respect the royal family and they are known world wide. They have amazing loyalty to the charities which they help and without their funding and hard work those charities would not be able to bring in as much money which is desperately needed.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

The history involved with the monarchy is also something we do different to other countries. People enjoy learning about it and getting involved with milestone events ie jubilee, weddings etc.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

Many people travel from around the world as tourists to visit places like Buckingham Palace so the royal family have a positive affect on tourism. I also like the fact that the monarchy and royal family have some control over the British government. Over all I think the monarchy is a positive thing for the UK.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

I am incredibly proud of our monarchy and what it stands for. Whilst I understand that their incredibly lavish lifestyle in predominantly funded from the public purse, I also believe that they raise a large amount of revenue for our country, be it through the good caused they champion but mostly through the tourism created by their global popularity and unique lifestyle. People travel from all over the world to see Buckingham Palace and spend their tourist cash in London. I believe they have an overall positive impact.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text:
It is time for the UK to move forward in the world and leave the powers of a Monarchy behind us. I do not see why a royal family would provide any use for the amount of wealth it keeps hold of, where as there should be a fairer gap in wealth with the rest of the country.

The royal family to not represent the rest of society and as far as I am aware, the royal family do not provide a positive affect to the economy. It may attract tourism but that is the only good thing I can think of.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

Their residences cost millions of pounds to maintain at great expense to the UK taxpayers - money which could be put to far better use. And all because they had the 'fortune' to be born into a family that heads the UK. I really cannot see what contribution they bring to us; they are leeches.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

Because of the length of time she had on the thrown and her professionalism throughout her reign, she was regarded very highly. However there has been a shift since Charles became King, my opinion included. I'm not sure abolishing is necessary but maybe an investigation or inquest on spending vs income.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

The monarchy is, however, a very traditional institution and doesn't really understand how the majority of the UK population lives, so they are pretty well irrelevant to most of us. I'm also fed up of the scandals from the family, divorces, SA accusations, the Sussexes who seem hell bent on causing issues.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.
rows.text: I'm not a huge fan of the monarchy, they cost the tax payer millions of pounds a year & live very well, but they do bring in vast amounts of tourism revenue. There's Putin & many others who totally disregard laws for their own wealth & ego's & I just worry that getting rid of the monarchy will start the descent, democracy will be lost & god help us then.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

rows.proposition: The UK should abolish the monarchy.

I haven't researched enough into the true heirs to the throne but I believe that the true British aristocracy would be British and not German/Greek.
